trivial_case.description: Decidability of the domino problem in 1D: given a finite set of allowed words / a 1D nearest-neighbor matching rule over a finite alphabet, it is decidable whether a valid bi-infinite tiling exists
trivial_case.result: 1D domino problem is decidable (reduces to checking nonemptiness of a finite directed graph)
trivial_case.proven_by: Folklore; explicit in early symbolic dynamics literature
trivial_case.venue: Standard symbolic dynamics; cf. Lind-Marcus, 'An Introduction to Symbolic Dynamics and Coding', Cambridge 1995, Chapter 2

open_case.description: Domino problem on hyperbolic groups and on specific Cayley graphs: classify which finitely generated groups have decidable domino problem (i.e., subshift-of-finite-type emptiness problem)
open_case.result: Decidable on Z (1D), undecidable on Z^d for d >= 2 (Berger 1966), undecidable on free groups of rank >= 2 (Aubrun-Barbieri-Moutot 2019), open on hyperbolic groups in general (and on Baumslag-Solitar groups for many parameters); full classification by group is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates 1D decidability (trivial) with 2D / general-group undecidability (Berger; open for many group classes)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish 1D (decidable) from Z^d (undecidable d>=2) and from general-group (open) versions of the domino problem

entries.conjecture_family: Easton-style continuum function on regular cardinals versus singulars

trivial_case.description: Easton's theorem: for any class function F : Regulars -> Cardinals satisfying F(kappa) > kappa, F monotonic, and cf(F(kappa)) > kappa, there is a forcing extension where 2^kappa = F(kappa) for all regular kappa (assuming GCH in the ground model)
trivial_case.result: Class forcing with Easton products produces extensions realizing any allowed continuum function on regulars
trivial_case.venue: Annals of Math. Logic 1 (1970), 'Powers of regular cardinals'

open_case.description: Characterization of all possible continuum functions on singular cardinals consistent with ZFC; PCF theory imposes nontrivial constraints (Shelah's bound 2^aleph_omega < aleph_{omega_4} under strong-limit) but the full characterization is open
open_case.result: Shelah's PCF gives upper bounds; consistency-strength results constrain failure of SCH; full characterization open
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; central singular-cardinals problem

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Easton-style theorem covers singulars (false — singulars are PCF-constrained); conflates regular-cardinal Easton with open singular case
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish Easton on regulars (1970) from open singular-cardinals continuum-function problem

entries.conjecture_family: Local smoothing for the wave equation on R^n

trivial_case.description: Local smoothing in 2D for the wave equation: e^{it sqrt{-Delta}} f satisfies sharp local-smoothing bounds
trivial_case.result: sharp 2D local smoothing
trivial_case.status: PROVEN (in n=2 to the conjectured endpoint via decoupling)

open_case.description: Sharp local smoothing for n >= 3: full conjectured range
open_case.result: partial (Bourgain-Demeter decoupling in 2015 gives the sharp loss at the L^p-endpoint with delta^{-epsilon} loss); sharp endpoint constants open in higher dim
open_case.status: OPEN at sharp endpoint in n >= 3

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model attributes local smoothing in all dimensions to a single result, conflating 2D Guth-Wang-Zhang with higher-dim partial bounds
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; 2D recently settled (2020), higher-dim still open

trivial_case.description: Erdős-Gallai theorem: graph G with average degree > k-2 contains every path on k vertices
trivial_case.result: tight bound for paths

open_case.description: Erdős-Sós conjecture: every graph with average degree > k-2 contains EVERY tree on k vertices (not just paths)
open_case.result: proven for sufficiently-large k by Ajtai, Komlós, Simonovits, Szemerédi (announced 2000s, manuscripts circulated; full publication delayed)
open_case.status: Resolved for large k; small-k cases reportedly open or only-recently-published

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Erdős-Sós is fully proven OR unaware of AKSS announcement
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; specific-tree (path) proven vs general-tree conjectured-and-mostly-proven

trivial_case.description: Hales-Jewett theorem (coloring version)
trivial_case.result: any r-coloring of [k]^N with N >= HJ(r,k) contains a monochromatic combinatorial line

open_case.description: Quantitative bounds on HJ(r,k) and density-Hales-Jewett bounds DHJ(k, delta)
open_case.result: Polymath 1 (2010) gave the first elementary combinatorial proof of DHJ; quantitative bounds remain weak (tower-type for k >= 3); polynomial bounds OPEN
open_case.status: Existence proven; sharp quantitative bounds OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Polymath 1 'proved DHJ' as if proving existence (Furstenberg-Katznelson 1991 already proved existence; Polymath gave the first elementary proof)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; elementary-proof-by-Polymath vs original-ergodic-proof-by-FK

trivial_case.description: Tree-like resolution lower bounds for pigeonhole / Tseitin
trivial_case.result: Exponential lower bounds proven via width-size tradeoff

open_case.description: Super-polynomial Frege lower bounds for explicit tautologies
open_case.result: Open; even polynomial-size Frege upper bound conjectures are not refuted

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: extrapolating resolution lower bounds to Frege
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: weak-system-LB-trivial vs strong-system-LB-open

trivial_case.description: K^c (the core model below a measurable) is constructible in ZFC + 'no inner model with a measurable cardinal'; covering lemma holds for K below 0†
trivial_case.result: Mitchell-Steel core-model construction K^c; Dodd-Jensen covering
trivial_case.proven_by: William Mitchell, John Steel, Anthony Dodd, Ronald Jensen
trivial_case.venue: Mitchell-Steel, 'Fine Structure and Iteration Trees', Lect. Notes Logic 3 (1994)

open_case.description: Existence of a fine-structural canonical inner model with a Woodin cardinal that is also a limit of Woodin cardinals + measurable, with full iterability proof; the 'core model induction' beyond AD_R + Theta-regular is open
open_case.result: Sargsyan's hod-mouse construction below a Woodin-limit-of-Woodins (2014, Memoirs AMS); above this level open; Nairian models (Sargsyan-Blue-Larson, arXiv 2501.18958, 2025) make further partial progress
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; active hod-mouse / inner-model-induction program

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims hod-mouse construction is complete through supercompact (false — only partial); conflates K^c (settled below measurable) with hod-mouse program (open above current frontier)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled core-model below measurable from open hod-mouse frontier

trivial_case.description: 2D incompressible Navier-Stokes: smooth solutions exist globally for smooth, finite-energy initial data
trivial_case.result: global regularity
trivial_case.proven_by: Leray (existence); Ladyzhenskaya / Lions (smoothness via L^infinity vorticity transport in 2D)

open_case.description: 3D incompressible Navier-Stokes: do smooth solutions exist globally for smooth, finite-energy initial data, OR can a finite-time singularity form?
open_case.result: Leray weak solutions exist (1934); CKN partial regularity (1982); ESS L^3 critical (2003); Tao averaged-NS blowup (2016); no resolution

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims NS regularity proven without distinguishing 2D from 3D, or attributes 3D regularity to Leray (who only got weak solutions)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; same equation different dimensions

trivial_case.description: Steiner triple systems STS(n) exist iff n ≡ 1 or 3 (mod 6)
trivial_case.result: necessary divisibility conditions are also sufficient

open_case.description: Existence of 1-factorizations of K_n for general perfect-1-factorization extensions; the Kotzig Perfect 1-Factorization Conjecture for K_n with n even and n >= 4
open_case.result: perfect 1-factorizations known for K_{p+1} (p prime) and many other orders, but conjectured to exist for ALL K_n with n even — open in general

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates 1-factorization existence (trivially every K_n with n even has a 1-factorization) with perfect 1-factorization (the open conjecture)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; basic-existence vs perfect-existence

trivial_case.description: Zarankiewicz problem for K_{2,2}-free bipartite graphs
trivial_case.status: PROVEN tight up to constants for (2,2) and (3,3)

open_case.description: Zarankiewicz z(n,n; s, t) for general s, t
open_case.result: Kővári-Sós-Turán 1954 gives upper bound O(n^{2 - 1/s}); tight only for (2,2), (3,3), and (s,t) with t > (s-1)! (Kollár-Rónyai-Szabó 1996, Alon-Rónyai-Szabó 1999); general tight bound OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims KST bound is tight in general
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; specific-cases-tight vs general-open

entries.conjecture_family: Furstenberg sets in R^2 (intermediate Hausdorff dimensions)

trivial_case.description: Marstrand projection theorem: for E in R^2 with dim_H E = s, almost every direction's projection P_theta E has dim_H min(s, 1)
trivial_case.result: Marstrand 1954 dim projection theorem

open_case.description: Furstenberg set conjecture in R^2: for s in [0,1], dim_H of an (s, 1-s)-Furstenberg set (set containing an s-dim subset of a line in every direction) is at least 1 + s
open_case.result: Bourgain 2003 conditional bounds; Wolff 1999 partial bounds; Orponen-Shmerkin 2023 (paraphrase) major progress; sharp form OPEN; recent strong results in some s ranges

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Furstenberg set conjecture is settled by Marstrand or by Wolff
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; subtle distinction between projection theorem and Furstenberg sets

trivial_case.description: F_2 (second moment) estimation in streaming uses Theta(log n) bits via AMS sketch (constant approximation, constant error)
trivial_case.result: Proven

open_case.description: Tight communication / streaming complexity for general F_p moments with arbitrary p > 2 in adversarial setting
open_case.result: Open in some regimes; specific cases (p > 2) need polynomial space (Bar-Yossef-Jayram-Kumar-Sivakumar 2002)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming AMS-style sketches generalize to all F_p
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: sketching tractability has p-threshold

trivial_case.description: 2-to-2 Games Conjecture: gap-NP-hardness for 2-to-2 constraint covering instances
trivial_case.result: Proven via pseudorandom-set expansion in the Grassmann graph

open_case.description: Full Unique Games Conjecture: gap-NP-hardness for 1-to-1 (unique) constraint covering instances
open_case.result: Open; the 1-to-1 lifting from KMS-2018 has not been completed

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming UGC is proven by KMS-2018 (it is not — only the 2-to-2 weakening)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish weakening-proven from full-conjecture-open within same family

trivial_case.description: Consistency strength of a measurable cardinal is strictly above ZFC: if kappa is measurable, then 0# exists, hence V != L, and one can prove Con(ZFC + 'there is a measurable cardinal') is strictly above Con(ZFC)
trivial_case.result: Scott's theorem: if there is a measurable, then V != L; combined with standard equiconsistency arguments
trivial_case.proven_by: Dana Scott (V != L) and Gaifman-Solovay (consistency-strength refinements)
trivial_case.venue: Scott, Bull. Acad. Polon. Sci. 9 (1961), 'Measurable cardinals and constructible sets'

open_case.description: Precise consistency-strength placement of Vopěnka's principle relative to other large cardinals: known to lie between extendible and almost-huge, but precise calibration with intermediate notions (e.g., C^{(n)}-extendibles, supercompact-of-Laver) remains under refinement
open_case.result: Bagaria's C^{(n)}-cardinal hierarchy (Archive Math Logic 2012) gives partial calibration; precise Vopěnka strength relative to specific large-cardinal axioms still being refined
open_case.status: OPEN refinements as of 2026; Vopěnka equivalent to 'C^{(n)}-extendibles for all n' but finer placements active

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Vopěnka strength is exactly characterized (it is up to a level); conflates measurable existence (settled-strict-above-ZFC) with Vopěnka calibration (open refinements)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled measurable-vs-ZFC strength jump from open precise Vopěnka calibration

trivial_case.description: Szemerédi's theorem: every positive-density subset of N contains arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions
trivial_case.result: for every k, r_k(N) = o(N)

open_case.description: Exact rate of r_k(N) for k >= 4; specifically the polylogarithmic-Behrend-conjecture exponent
open_case.result: Bloom-Sisask, Leng-Sah-Sawhney 2024 made progress; the conjectured rate r_k(N) ~ N exp(-(log N)^c) for c depending on k is partially established
open_case.status: OPEN for tight constants when k >= 4

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model presents Szemerédi as 'fully solving' k-AP problem, ignoring the rate question
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; existence-proven (Szemerédi) vs sharp-rate-open

entries.conjecture_family: Circuit lower bounds for non-uniform classes

trivial_case.description: NEXP not in non-uniform ACC^0
trivial_case.result: Proven via non-trivial-savings algorithm for ACC^0 SAT

open_case.description: NEXP not in non-uniform P/poly
open_case.result: Open; would require nontrivial savings for P/poly SAT, which becomes the new open problem

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: conflating NEXP-vs-ACC^0 with NEXP-vs-P/poly, claiming Williams's result implies the stronger separation
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: scope of proven result vs scope of open problem

trivial_case.description: NPSPACE = PSPACE (Savitch's theorem: NSPACE(s) ⊆ DSPACE(s^2))
trivial_case.result: Proven

open_case.description: P = PSPACE? (and the related L vs P, NL vs P, P vs NP)
open_case.result: Open; relativization barrier (BGS 1975) applies

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming Savitch's theorem implies anything about P vs PSPACE
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: nondeterminism-within-space cheap; time-vs-space gap hard

trivial_case.description: Stable ergodicity in the Anosov case: a volume-preserving Anosov diffeomorphism of a closed manifold is stably ergodic - any C^1-small volume-preserving perturbation is ergodic
trivial_case.result: Stable ergodicity for Anosov volume-preserving diffeomorphisms
trivial_case.venue: Anosov, 'Geodesic flows on closed Riemannian manifolds with negative curvature', Trudy Mat. Inst. Steklov 90 (1967)

open_case.description: Pugh-Shub stable ergodicity conjecture for partially hyperbolic systems: stable ergodicity is C^r-dense (r >= 2) among C^r volume-preserving partially hyperbolic diffeomorphisms with the accessibility property
open_case.result: Pugh-Shub C^1 density established (Burns-Wilkinson 2010 for center-bunched case proved sufficient conditions); C^r density for r >= 2 of stable ergodicity in the partially hyperbolic class is OPEN; the C^1-essential-accessibility conjecture and related sharper forms are OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Anosov / Burns-Wilkinson as 'establishing stable ergodicity for partially hyperbolic systems' without distinguishing Anosov (proven) from partially-hyperbolic-C^r (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish Anosov stable ergodicity (proven) from partially hyperbolic Pugh-Shub conjecture (open), and within the latter distinguish C^1 (partial) from C^r, r >= 2 (open)

entries.conjecture_family: Smale's stability conjectures and density of hyperbolicity

trivial_case.description: C^1 structural stability conjecture for diffeomorphisms: a C^1 diffeomorphism of a closed manifold is C^1 structurally stable iff it satisfies Axiom A and the strong transversality condition
trivial_case.result: C^1 structural stability is equivalent to Axiom A + strong transversality
trivial_case.proven_by: Mane (sufficient direction by Robbin and Robinson earlier; full equivalence by Mane)
trivial_case.venue: Mane, 'A proof of the C^1 stability conjecture', Publ. IHES 66 (1988), 161-210

open_case.description: C^r structural stability conjecture for r >= 2: same equivalence in C^r topology
open_case.result: Even C^2 structural stability conjecture is open; only the C^1 case is settled

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model treats Mane's theorem as resolving 'the structural stability conjecture' across all C^r, when only C^1 is proved
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish topology of perturbation (C^1 vs C^r, r >= 2) within the same conjecture

trivial_case.description: ZFC proves cf(2^aleph_omega) > aleph_omega: the cofinality of the continuum function at a singular is bounded below by the cofinality of the cardinal itself (Konig's theorem applied to singular case)
trivial_case.result: Konig's inequality kappa^{cf(kappa)} > kappa applied to 2^aleph_omega
trivial_case.venue: Math. Ann. 60 (1905); standard exposition in Jech 'Set Theory'

open_case.description: Whether 2^aleph_omega can equal aleph_{omega_1+omega} or aleph_{omega_1} or some specific value beyond aleph_{omega_1}; whether the Shelah Strong Hypothesis pp(aleph_omega) < aleph_{omega_1} holds
open_case.result: Shelah's PCF gives upper bound aleph_{omega_4}; Magidor-Shelah forcing constructions give consistency of various values < aleph_{omega_4}; precise ZFC value unknown

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims 2^aleph_omega has a known ZFC value (false), or conflates Konig's lemma (settled) with PCF bound problem (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish ZFC-trivial Konig bound from open PCF strong-hypothesis question

entries.conjecture_family: Williams' shift-equivalence program for subshifts of finite type

trivial_case.description: Reducible / specific subclass: shift equivalence implies strong shift equivalence for irreducible SFTs over {0,1} matrices of small size, and for specific structured matrix families where the obstruction does not appear
trivial_case.result: Williams' SE-implies-SSE conjecture is FALSE in general for nonnegative integer matrices
trivial_case.venue: Kim-Roush, 'The Williams conjecture is false for irreducible subshifts', Annals of Math. 149 (1999), 545-558

open_case.description: Classification of irreducible SFTs up to topological conjugacy: is shift equivalence (or some computable invariant) sufficient to classify irreducible SFTs up to conjugacy?
open_case.result: No computable complete invariant for SFT conjugacy is known; shift equivalence is decidable but does not classify; classification problem is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Kim-Roush 'solved Williams' conjecture' as if it positively classified SFTs, missing that it refuted the proposed invariant and left the classification open
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish a refutation of a proposed invariant (Kim-Roush) from a positive classification result (still open)

entries.conjecture_family: Polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa over R^n / harmonic-analysis context

trivial_case.description: Polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa (PFR) over F_2^n
trivial_case.result: proven Gowers-Green-Manners-Tao 2023

open_case.description: Polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa over Z (integer-valued) and over R^n at the additive-combinatorics endpoint
open_case.result: Z partial; the Z (and R^n) PFR remains an active research area; conjectured polynomial bound vs Plunnecke-Ruzsa in some forms still open
open_case.status: OPEN over Z and (in stronger forms) over R^n

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims PFR is 'fully proven by GGMT' without the F_2^n restriction
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; recent breakthrough in F_2^n, Z still open

entries.conjecture_family: Boltzmann-Grad limit and Hilbert's 6th problem (kinetic limits)

trivial_case.description: Boltzmann-Grad limit for a hard-sphere gas on a short time interval: in the Boltzmann-Grad scaling, the empirical density of N hard spheres converges in the limit N -> infinity to a solution of the Boltzmann equation, on a time interval shorter than the mean free time
trivial_case.result: Lanford's theorem: validity of the Boltzmann equation from hard-sphere dynamics for short times
trivial_case.venue: Lanford, 'Time evolution of large classical systems', in 'Dynamical Systems, Theory and Applications', Springer LNP 38 (1975), 1-111

open_case.description: Boltzmann-Grad limit valid for all times (or for arbitrarily long times): rigorously derive the Boltzmann equation from N-particle hard-sphere dynamics for time scales beyond Lanford's, and obtain irreversibility / H-theorem
open_case.result: Gallagher-Saint-Raymond-Texier 2014 made Lanford rigorous and extended techniques; Bodineau-Gallagher-Saint-Raymond-Simonella obtained results for linearized Boltzmann around equilibrium for longer times; the full long-time Hilbert 6th derivation is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Lanford as 'rigorously deriving the Boltzmann equation', without flagging the short-time hypothesis
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish short-time validity (Lanford) from long-time / global derivation (open)

trivial_case.description: AC^0[p] lower bounds for prime p
trivial_case.result: MAJORITY (and MOD_q for q != p coprime to p) are not in AC^0[p]; proven via approximation by low-degree polynomials over F_p

open_case.description: Specific lower bounds in AC^0[m] for composite m beyond ACC^0 statement; specifically TC^0 lower bounds for explicit functions
open_case.result: Open; even depth-3 threshold-circuit lower bounds for explicit functions are weak (Hajnal-Maass-Pudlak-Szegedy-Turan 1993 give n-input lower bounds but not super-poly)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming Razborov-Smolensky covers AC^0[m] for composite m
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: prime-modulus technique does not extend to composite

trivial_case.description: Ergodicity of geodesic flow on closed surfaces of constant negative curvature: the geodesic flow on the unit tangent bundle of a closed hyperbolic surface is ergodic (in fact mixing of all orders) with respect to Liouville measure
trivial_case.result: Hopf's theorem on ergodicity of geodesic flow in constant negative curvature, extended by Anosov to variable negative curvature
trivial_case.venue: Hopf, 'Statistik der geodatischen Linien in Mannigfaltigkeiten negativer Krummung', Berichte Verh. Sachs. Akad. Wiss. Leipzig 91 (1939), 261-304; Anosov, Trudy Mat. Inst. Steklov 90 (1967)

open_case.description: Ergodicity of generic Hamiltonian / billiard systems with positive Lyapunov exponents: for a generic smooth convex billiard table in R^2, is the billiard map ergodic with respect to Liouville measure on the phase space, away from caustics?
open_case.result: Sinai 1970 proved ergodicity for dispersing billiards; Bunimovich for stadium-type; for generic smooth convex (focusing) billiards the ergodicity question is OPEN (and known to fail for circular tables and small perturbations preserving caustics, by Lazutkin's KAM-type result)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Hopf-Anosov as resolving 'ergodicity of Hamiltonian flows', missing that generic smooth convex billiards are open and can have KAM-type non-ergodic regions
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish negative-curvature geodesic flows (Hopf-Anosov, ergodic) from generic-billiard / mixed-phase-space (open) within the ergodicity-of-Hamiltonian-flow program

trivial_case.description: Worst-case to average-case reductions for permanent (Lipton 1989) and other random-self-reducible problems
trivial_case.result: Proven; permanent is random-self-reducible — solving permanent on a 1/poly fraction of inputs gives worst-case algorithm

open_case.description: Worst-case to average-case reductions for NP problems (e.g., 3-SAT) — random-self-reducibility for NP-complete problems
open_case.result: Open; Bogdanov-Trevisan 2006 showed barriers for non-adaptive reductions

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming permanent's worst-case-to-average-case extends to NP
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: algebraic-vs-Boolean random-self-reducibility distinction

trivial_case.description: Polynomial Szemeredi theorem: for any subset of integers of positive upper density and any polynomials p_1,...,p_k in Z[x] with p_i(0)=0, the set contains a configuration {a, a+p_1(d), ..., a+p_k(d)} for some d != 0
trivial_case.result: Polynomial Szemeredi holds via ergodic-theoretic methods (PET induction)
trivial_case.venue: Bergelson-Leibman, 'Polynomial extensions of van der Waerden's and Szemeredi's theorems', J. Amer. Math. Soc. 9 (1996), 725-753

open_case.description: Effective / quantitative polynomial Szemeredi: for the polynomial pattern {a, a+d^2}, give a Behrend-type lower bound and a polynomial-strength upper bound on the density of subsets of [N] avoiding it
open_case.result: Sarkozy 1978 gave qualitative version; quantitative bounds of strength matching the linear (Szemeredi) regime, and polynomial-Szemeredi with effective bounds for general nonlinear configurations, remain OPEN (best known bounds are far from tight)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model treats Bergelson-Leibman as giving quantitative bounds, conflating qualitative existence (proven) with effective rates (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish qualitative ergodic existence from effective combinatorial rates within the same family

trivial_case.description: NL = co-NL (NSPACE-class is closed under complement)
trivial_case.result: Proven via inductive counting

open_case.description: NP = co-NP?
open_case.result: Open; widely conjectured FALSE

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming Immerman-Szelepcsenyi technique extends to NP
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: space-vs-time complement-closure asymmetry

entries.conjecture_family: Calderon problem (recovery of conductivity from Dirichlet-to-Neumann data)

trivial_case.description: n = 2: sufficiently smooth conductivities recoverable from boundary DtN data
trivial_case.result: uniqueness + reconstruction

open_case.description: n >= 3: full uniqueness for L^infinity conductivities
open_case.result: smooth conductivities settled (Sylvester-Uhlmann 1987); rough cases partial - uniqueness for C^1 by Haberman-Tataru 2013, partial L^infinity progress; full L^infinity OPEN in n>=3
open_case.status: OPEN in full L^infinity range for n >= 3

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Calderon problem is 'solved by Astala-Paivarinta' without specifying 2D restriction
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; dimension-dependent settledness

trivial_case.description: Janson inequality for lower-tail of subgraph counts in G(n,p)
trivial_case.result: tight exponential lower-tail bound for subgraph indicators

open_case.description: Sharp upper-tail rate for triangle counts in G(n,p)
open_case.result: Lubetzky-Zhao 2017 / Harel-Mousset-Samotij 2019/2022 (arxiv 1904.08212) gave matching upper and lower bounds for the upper-tail rate function for triangles and many subgraph counts; some refined 'large deviation rate' questions remain technically open
open_case.status: Mostly resolved 2017-2022; some refined cases remain

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims upper-tail problem is wide open (it was resolved 2017-2022 for triangles and many subgraphs)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; triangle-upper-tail-recently-closed vs general-open-status

trivial_case.description: Anosov diffeomorphisms on tori: every Anosov diffeomorphism of T^n is topologically conjugate to a hyperbolic toral automorphism (a linear Anosov)
trivial_case.result: Classification on infranilmanifolds modeled on tori; specifically Franks-Manning theorem on tori
trivial_case.venue: Franks, 'Anosov diffeomorphisms', Proc. Sympos. Pure Math. 14 (1970), 61-93; Manning, 'There are no new Anosov diffeomorphisms on tori', Amer. J. Math. 96 (1974), 422-429

open_case.description: Anosov diffeomorphisms on general closed manifolds: is every closed manifold supporting an Anosov diffeomorphism necessarily an infranilmanifold (Smale's question)?
open_case.result: Known on tori and infranilmanifolds, and on rational homology spheres (Manning, Brin-Manning extensions); the general question - whether all Anosov manifolds are infranilmanifolds - is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model treats Franks-Manning as classifying all Anosov diffeomorphisms, missing that the underlying-manifold question is open
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish 'on a torus' (classified) from 'on a general manifold' (open) within the Anosov family

trivial_case.description: 2D and 3D pure Yang-Mills: existence of the quantum theory has rigorous constructive control (super-renormalizable)
trivial_case.result: existence in 2D and 3D
trivial_case.proven_by: various constructive QFT (Glimm-Jaffe program, Magnen-Rivasseau, Balaban for partial 3D)
trivial_case.status: PROVEN (with technical caveats on 3D pure non-abelian)

open_case.description: 4D pure Yang-Mills: rigorous existence + mass gap (Clay Millennium problem)
open_case.result: no rigorous construction; lattice numerical evidence overwhelming

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Yang-Mills is 'a solved theory' or attributes 4D mass gap to lattice computations as if those constitute proof
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; renormalizability distinguishes super-renormalizable (lower-dim) from marginal (4D)

trivial_case.description: Roth's theorem: every positive-density subset of N contains a 3-AP

open_case.description: Exact rate of decay of r_3(N)/N
open_case.result: Kelley-Meka 2023 (arxiv 2302.05537): r_3(N) <= N exp(-C(log N)^{1/12}); Behrend lower bound r_3(N) >= N exp(-C sqrt(log N)); gap from (log N)^{1/12} to (log N)^{1/2}

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Kelley-Meka or Bloom-Sisask 'solved Roth' (they sharpened the upper bound; the matching lower bound was already near-Behrend in 1946)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; existence-proven vs sharp-rate-open

entries.conjecture_family: Kakeya conjecture (Hausdorff dimension of Besicovitch sets)

trivial_case.description: n = 2: every plane Besicovitch set has Hausdorff dimension 2
trivial_case.result: dim_H = 2

open_case.description: n >= 3: every Besicovitch set in R^n has Hausdorff dimension n
open_case.result: best lower bounds: dim_H >= (n+2)/2 (Wolff 1995); for n=3, dim_H >= 5/2 + epsilon (small explicit, Wang-Zahl 2022 arxiv 2207.01054)
open_case.status: OPEN; bounds well below n for n >= 3

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Kakeya is 'proven in all dimensions' or 'proven by Davies' without distinguishing dim 2 from higher
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5 / FM-08 architectural pattern; substrate must distinguish proven dim=2 from open n>=3

entries.conjecture_family: Forcing-axiom strength at omega_1 versus higher cardinals

trivial_case.description: MA_{aleph_1} (Martin's Axiom at aleph_1) is consistent with ZFC and implies 2^aleph_0 > aleph_1; the standard ccc iteration of length aleph_2 over a model of GCH yields a model of MA_{aleph_1} + 2^aleph_0 = aleph_2
trivial_case.result: Solovay-Tennenbaum forcing iteration produces MA + ¬CH
trivial_case.venue: Annals of Mathematics 94 (1971), 'Iterated Cohen extensions and Souslin's problem'

open_case.description: A satisfactory two-cardinal forcing axiom at omega_2 with the strength of MA at omega_1 — i.e., a generalization MA(aleph_2) preserving aleph_2 with full ccc-analogue — remains open; Shelah's MA(aleph_2) variants and Neeman's two-cardinal proper forcing only partially resolve this
open_case.result: Shelah's MA^+ at aleph_2, Neeman's two-cardinal side-condition method give partial substitutes; full natural analogue open
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; active program (Neeman, Veličković, Aspero)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims MA(aleph_2) is a known theorem analogous to MA at aleph_1; conflates the omega_1 and omega_2 cases
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled MA_{aleph_1} (Solovay-Tennenbaum 1971) from open higher-cardinal analogues

trivial_case.description: Kunen's inconsistency theorem: in ZFC, there is no nontrivial elementary embedding j : V -> V; equivalently, no Reinhardt cardinal exists in ZFC
trivial_case.result: Kunen used the Erdos-Hajnal partition theorem to derive contradiction from j : V -> V in ZFC
trivial_case.venue: Kunen, J. Symb. Logic 36 (1971), 'Elementary embeddings and infinitary combinatorics'

open_case.description: Whether ZF + 'there is a Reinhardt cardinal' is consistent (without Choice); whether ZF + Berkeley cardinals is consistent; whether choiceless large cardinals beyond ZFC have a coherent inner-model theory
open_case.result: Bagaria-Koellner-Woodin (2019) established consistency strength of various choiceless large cardinals relative to each other; absolute consistency relative to ZF (or ZF + small fragments) remains open. Goldberg's recent work (2021-2024) on choiceless cardinals continues

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Reinhardt cardinals are inconsistent in ZF (only proven in ZFC); conflates Kunen ZFC-inconsistency (settled) with ZF-consistency (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled Kunen-inconsistency-in-ZFC from open ZF-without-Choice version

entries.conjecture_family: Nivat / Pansiot conjecture on complexity of multidimensional subshifts

trivial_case.description: 1D Morse-Hedlund theorem: a one-sided sequence over a finite alphabet is eventually periodic iff its complexity function p(n) (number of distinct length-n factors) is bounded, equivalently iff p(n) <= n for some n
trivial_case.result: Morse-Hedlund characterization of periodicity via complexity in 1D

open_case.description: Nivat's conjecture (2D): if a 2D configuration over a finite alphabet has rectangular complexity p(m, n) <= mn for some m, n >= 1, then it is periodic (admits a nontrivial translation invariance)
open_case.result: Cyr-Kra proved p(m, n) <= mn/2 implies periodicity; Kari-Szabados proved algebraic versions; full Nivat with the original mn bound is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model treats Morse-Hedlund as covering the multidimensional case, or cites Cyr-Kra as resolving Nivat with the original constant
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish 1D Morse-Hedlund (proven) from the 2D Nivat conjecture (open) within the complexity-vs-periodicity family

trivial_case.description: k-uniform intersecting family on [n] with n >= 2k
trivial_case.result: max size is C(n-1, k-1) (the 'star' bound)

open_case.description: Frankl's union-closed conjecture: every union-closed family of sets contains an element in at least half the sets
open_case.result: best known constant approx 0.38197 (Sawin/Cambie/Chase-Lovett 2022, building on Gilmer)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates EKR (intersection) with union-closed (union closure) families OR gives 1/2 bound as proven
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: trivially-proven (EKR star bound) vs open (union-closed half-element) within adjacent set-system territory

trivial_case.description: PCP theorem with constant soundness gap (any constant gap suffices for NP-hardness of approximation)
trivial_case.result: Proven

open_case.description: Sliding-scale conjecture (Bellare-Goldwasser-Lund-Russell): for every constant alpha, alpha-gap PCPs of polynomial size
open_case.result: Open; partial results, large gaps known to require exponential proof length under standard PCP

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming PCP theorem covers sliding-scale conjecture
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: existence-of-some-gap vs all-constant-gaps distinction

trivial_case.description: L^p boundedness of the Hilbert transform on R for 1 < p < infinity
trivial_case.result: bounded on L^p with sharp constants known

open_case.description: Sharp constants for the Hilbert transform on weighted L^p spaces with general A_p weights
open_case.result: A_2 conjecture (||T||_{L^2(w)} <= C [w]_{A_2}) proven by Hytonen 2012 Annals for general CZ operators; some endpoint refinements still open
open_case.status: MAIN A_2 conjecture PROVEN 2012; some endpoint refinements OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims the A_2 conjecture is 'still open' (it was settled in 2012)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; conjecture name persistent in literature, but resolved 2012

trivial_case.description: Disjointness has Theta(n) deterministic and Theta(n) randomized communication complexity
trivial_case.result: Proven

open_case.description: Quantum communication complexity vs randomized: largest possible exponential separation for explicit total functions
open_case.result: Open; specific functions show separations (Raz 1999) but tight characterization for total functions is open

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming the partial-function separations imply total-function separations
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: total-vs-partial-function gap

trivial_case.description: Schur's theorem: any finite coloring of N has a monochromatic x + y = z
trivial_case.result: for any r-coloring of [N] with N >= S(r), there exist x, y, z with same color and x + y = z

open_case.description: Polynomial Schur / nonlinear Schur: monochromatic solutions to x + y = z^2 etc.
open_case.result: Frantzikinakis-Host 2018 and follow-ups gave density versions for some equations; specific equations like x + y = z^2 in N have density-monochromatic solutions; full polynomial-Schur OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims polynomial Schur is fully solved or that linear Schur covers it
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; linear-Schur-classical vs polynomial-Schur-open

entries.conjecture_family: Universally Baire sets and generic absoluteness

trivial_case.description: Under proper-class-many Woodin cardinals, the theory of L(R) is generically absolute: for every set forcing P, L(R)^V == L(R)^{V[G]} elementarily (for projective formulas, and more)
trivial_case.result: Woodin's generic-absoluteness theorem, using stationary-tower forcing and Sigma^2_1-absoluteness
trivial_case.venue: Steel, Notes on Woodin's lectures; published Woodin, 'Supercompact cardinals, sets of reals, and weakly homogeneous trees', PNAS 85 (1988)

open_case.description: Whether 'every set of reals is universally Baire' has the same consistency strength as 'AD_R + Theta is regular' or as 'AD^+ + V=L(P(R))'; the precise calibration of universal-Baireness with the Solovay-Theta hierarchy is open
open_case.result: Larson-Sargsyan-Trang (2024) established partial reductions; Schlutzenberg-Trang on derived models. Precise equiconsistency of all-sets-uB with specific AD+ axioms not fully resolved
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; arXiv 2106.04244 'consistency strength of determinacy when all sets are universally Baire' is partial progress

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims all-sets-uB equiconsistency with specific AD-variants is settled (only partial reductions known); conflates settled L(R)-generic-absoluteness with open uB-equiconsistency
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled L(R)-generic-absoluteness (Woodin 1988) from open all-sets-uB equiconsistency

trivial_case.description: Under SETH, edit distance and longest common subsequence require time n^{2 - o(1)}
trivial_case.result: Proven

open_case.description: Unconditional (without SETH) tight lower bounds on edit distance, LCS, OV
open_case.result: Open; would require resolving SETH

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the SETH-conditional bound is unconditional
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: conditional-vs-unconditional separation

entries.conjecture_family: Dispersive estimates for nonlinear Schrodinger / wave at the energy-critical exponent

trivial_case.description: Defocusing energy-critical NLS on R^n in n >= 3: global existence and scattering for radial data, smooth data, or small mass-critical data
trivial_case.result: global well-posedness + scattering
trivial_case.proven_by: Bourgain (radial 3D and 4D 1999); Colliander-Keel-Staffilani-Takaoka-Tao (3D, 2008); Visan (5D and higher, 2007)

open_case.description: Focusing energy-critical NLS at the threshold (mass = ground-state mass)
open_case.result: Kenig-Merle 2006 type concentration-compactness gives global existence below threshold; threshold and above-threshold dynamics partially open
open_case.status: OPEN above-threshold for general data; some specific dynamics characterizations recent (Duyckaerts-Kenig-Merle)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims energy-critical NLS is 'fully solved' or fails to distinguish defocusing (settled) from focusing (partially open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; sign of nonlinearity flips status

trivial_case.description: The Continuum Hypothesis 2^aleph_0 = aleph_1 is independent of ZFC: Goedel showed Con(ZFC) implies Con(ZFC + CH); Cohen showed Con(ZFC) implies Con(ZFC + ¬CH)
trivial_case.result: Independence of CH from ZFC, established via L (Goedel 1940) and forcing (Cohen 1963)
trivial_case.venue: Cohen, PNAS 50 (1963) and 51 (1964), 'The independence of the continuum hypothesis'

open_case.description: What is the 'correct' value of 2^aleph_0 under a privileged extension of ZFC? Woodin's program suggests aleph_2 via Pmax/(*); Goedel's program seeks intrinsically motivated axioms. No consensus on the value
open_case.result: Multiple programmatic candidates (Woodin's (*), forcing-axiom approach via MM++ implies (*), inner-model approaches) but no community-accepted determination
open_case.status: OPEN as a foundational/philosophical question; (*) follows from MM++ (Aspero-Schindler 2021), but the meta-question of which axiomatic extension is correct remains contested

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims CH is open within ZFC (it is not — it is independent), or claims continuum value is settled at aleph_2 (it is not — only conditional on chosen axiom)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled independence (Cohen 1963) from open programmatic determination of c

trivial_case.description: BPP ⊆ P/poly (Adleman) and BPP ⊆ Sigma_2 ∩ Pi_2 (Sipser-Lautemann)
trivial_case.result: Proven (non-uniform circuit upper bound and PH containment)

open_case.description: BPP = P (full unconditional derandomization)
open_case.result: Open; conditional under circuit lower bounds (Impagliazzo-Wigderson 1997)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: conflating P/poly containment with P containment
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: non-uniform-vs-uniform distinction

entries.conjecture_family: Beilinson-Bloch / regulator-period conjecture connections to L-values

trivial_case.description: Dirichlet class number formula: residue of L(s, chi) at s=1 expresses class numbers of corresponding number fields
trivial_case.result: explicit formula

open_case.description: Beilinson conjecture: special L-values L(s, M) at non-critical integers expressed via regulators of motivic cohomology
open_case.result: proven for specific cases (CM elliptic curves, products thereof, some Shimura varieties); general OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates Dirichlet's classical formula with the Beilinson conjecture's full generality
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; classical analytic-side known, motivic generalization open

trivial_case.description: log-rank lower bound: D(f) >= log_2 rank(M_f), where M_f is the communication matrix and D is deterministic CC
trivial_case.result: Trivially proven (rank bound is folklore)

open_case.description: Lovasz-Saks log-rank conjecture: D(f) <= polylog(rank(M_f))
open_case.result: Open; best known D(f) <= O(sqrt(rank) log rank) (Lovett 2016)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the log-rank conjecture is solved (only the trivial direction is)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: lower-bound side trivial, upper-bound side hard

trivial_case.description: Aubry-Mather sets for monotone twist maps of the annulus: for every rotation number alpha (rational or irrational), there exists an invariant Aubry-Mather set with that rotation number
trivial_case.result: Existence of Aubry-Mather sets for all rotation numbers in twist maps
trivial_case.venue: Mather, 'Existence of quasi-periodic orbits for twist homeomorphisms of the annulus', Topology 21 (1982), 457-467; Aubry-LeDaeron, 'The discrete Frenkel-Kontorova model and its extensions', Physica D 8 (1983), 381-422

open_case.description: Mather's connecting orbits / Arnold diffusion via Mather sets in higher-dimensional Tonelli systems: for a generic Tonelli Hamiltonian on T*T^n with n >= 3, there exist orbits with prescribed itineraries through Aubry-Mather sets at distinct cohomology classes
open_case.result: Mather 2003 announced; Cheng-Yan 2004, Kaloshin-Zhang and others established important cases; the full generic Mather connecting-orbits / Arnold-diffusion conjecture for n >= 3 is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates 2D twist-map Aubry-Mather (proven) with higher-dim Mather connecting orbits (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish dimension and existence-of-set vs connecting-orbits within the Aubry-Mather program

trivial_case.description: Weyl equidistribution for polynomial sequences: for any polynomial p(n) = a_k n^k + ... + a_1 n + a_0 with at least one a_j (j >= 1) irrational, the sequence (p(n) mod 1) is equidistributed on [0,1)
trivial_case.result: Polynomial Weyl equidistribution
trivial_case.venue: Weyl, 'Uber die Gleichverteilung von Zahlen mod. Eins', Math. Annalen 77 (1916), 313-352

open_case.description: Sharp discrepancy for the sequence (alpha n^2 mod 1) with alpha algebraic of degree 2: prove an upper bound D_N << N^{-1/2 + epsilon} on the discrepancy
open_case.result: Trivial bound D_N << N^{-c} for tiny c follows from Weyl differencing; bounds of strength N^{-1/4} known (Heath-Brown for some cases); the conjectured square-root cancellation N^{-1/2 + epsilon} for individual quadratic alpha is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model treats Weyl 1916 as giving sharp discrepancy bounds, conflating equidistribution (qualitative) with sharp discrepancy (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish equidistribution from discrepancy quantitative bounds

trivial_case.description: Area-minimizing 2-dimensional integral currents (or hypersurfaces) in R^3
trivial_case.result: smooth (real-analytic) away from a finite set of singular points (Fleming, Reifenberg, Almgren in increasing generality)
trivial_case.proven_by: Fleming-Federer (regularity of mass-minimizing 2-currents); De Giorgi (smoothness in n<=7 for hypersurfaces)

open_case.description: Almgren's regularity for area-minimizing m-dimensional currents in R^{m+k} for m >= 2, k >= 2: singular set has Hausdorff dimension at most m-2
open_case.result: Almgren 1986 monograph proved m-2 dim; recent simplifications (De Lellis-Spadaro 2014-2016 series); SHARPER bounds on the singular set's structure (e.g., (m-2)-rectifiable, etc.) partially open; specific configurations open
open_case.status: Almgren m-2 dim PROVEN; SHARPER structure partially OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Almgren's regularity is 'fully open' or 'fully solved' without distinguishing the dimension bound from sharper structure questions
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; main result proven, refinement open

trivial_case.description: Polynomial-method upper bound: every 3-AP-free subset of F_3^n has size at most c^n with c = 2.7559...
trivial_case.result: exponential improvement over earlier o(3^n / n) Bateman-Katz bound

open_case.description: Exact asymptotic constant c such that max cap-set in F_3^n is c^{n+o(n)}
open_case.result: current bounds 2.21^n (Edel lower-bound construction lift) <= max <= 2.7559^n
open_case.status: OPEN — gap of 0.5 in the exponent base

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims c = 2.7559 is the EXACT asymptotic, conflating upper bound with tight constant
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; bounded-with-known-gap vs exact-constant

trivial_case.description: omega <= 2.376 (Coppersmith-Winograd)
trivial_case.result: Proven via tensor-decomposition + laser method
trivial_case.status: PROVEN; current best ~ 2.371552 by Williams-Xu-Xu-Zhou 2024 line

open_case.description: omega = 2 (Strassen's conjecture)
open_case.result: Open; trivial lower bound omega >= 2

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the current upper bound is the tight value
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: record-bound-vs-conjectured-value distinction

trivial_case.description: Arithmetic QUE: Hecke eigenforms on the modular surface have eigenfunctions equidistribute with respect to the hyperbolic measure
trivial_case.result: arithmetic QUE proven for compact arithmetic surfaces

open_case.description: Full QUE conjecture for general negatively curved surfaces (without arithmetic structure)
open_case.result: Anantharaman-Nonnenmacher 2007 partial entropy bounds; full QUE without arithmetic OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model attributes general QUE to Lindenstrauss without distinguishing arithmetic from general
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; arithmetic substrate gives proof leverage that general lacks

trivial_case.description: Hindman's theorem (HT): for every finite coloring of N, there is an infinite set X subset N such that all finite sums of distinct elements of X are monochromatic. HT implies ACA_0 over RCA_0 (Blass-Hirst-Simpson 1987)
trivial_case.result: Blass-Hirst-Simpson via syntactic-arithmetic reduction; HT is known to lie between ACA_0 and ACA_0^+
trivial_case.proven_by: Andreas Blass, Jeffry Hirst, Stephen Simpson
trivial_case.venue: Blass-Hirst-Simpson, 'Logical analysis of some theorems of combinatorics and topological dynamics', Contemp. Math. 65 (1987)
trivial_case.status: PROVEN (HT lower bound at ACA_0 and upper bound at ACA_0^+)

open_case.description: The exact reverse-mathematics strength of Hindman's theorem: whether HT is equivalent to ACA_0 or to ACA_0^+ over RCA_0; Towsner and others have made partial progress, but the precise calibration is open
open_case.result: Towsner (2012), Dzhafarov-Hirschfeldt-Lerman, and recent refinements narrow but do not close the ACA_0 vs ACA_0^+ gap
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; major problem in reverse mathematics

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims HT is equivalent to ACA_0 over RCA_0 (only one direction known precisely); conflates Hindman-implies-ACA_0 (settled) with precise-strength-determination (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled Hindman-implies-ACA_0 from open precise upper-bound calibration

trivial_case.description: Mass-critical defocusing NLS on R^n, n >= 1: global well-posedness + scattering for ALL data in L^2
trivial_case.result: global existence + scattering

open_case.description: Mass-critical FOCUSING NLS scattering above threshold mass
open_case.result: Killip-Visan-Zhang and others: scattering proven for radial data below ground state; non-radial above-threshold OPEN
open_case.status: OPEN above-ground-state mass for non-radial focusing

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims mass-critical NLS scattering is 'fully solved by Dodson' without distinguishing focusing from defocusing or radial threshold
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; sign of nonlinearity + radial restriction flip status

entries.conjecture_family: Ryser / Brualdi conjecture on Latin square transversals

trivial_case.description: Existence of partial transversals in Latin squares of order n
trivial_case.result: Hatami-Shor 2008: every Latin square of order n has a partial transversal of size n - O(log^2 n)

open_case.description: Ryser's conjecture: every Latin square of odd order has a transversal
open_case.result: Montgomery-Pokrovskiy-Sudakov 2020 proved every Latin square has a transversal of size n - 1 for sufficiently large n; full Ryser conjecture (transversal of size n for odd order) and Brualdi-Stein conjecture (transversal of size n - 1 always) — Brualdi recently resolved; Ryser may have been resolved by Montgomery (announcement); verify before quoting closure
open_case.status: Recent progress; verify exact closure status

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Ryser is fully solved or fully open without acknowledging the recent rapid progress 2020-2024
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; partial-transversal vs full-transversal; recent-rapid-progress regime

trivial_case.description: Erdős-Rényi connectivity threshold
trivial_case.result: G(n,p) is connected with high probability iff np - log n -> infinity

open_case.description: Shamir's perfect-matching threshold for random k-uniform hypergraphs (k >= 3)
open_case.result: Kahn 2023 (post-Park-Pham) confirmed Shamir's threshold conjecture for perfect matchings in random hypergraphs; before that it was known only via Park-Pham machinery
open_case.status: Recently CLOSED for many cases; some hypergraph-Shamir variants remain open

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model treats Shamir as still-open or as 'recently proven by Kahn-Kalai' (Kahn-Kalai gave the conjecture; Park-Pham 2022 proved the framework; Kahn 2023 + others closed specific cases)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; classical-threshold vs Shamir-style hypergraph-threshold

trivial_case.description: Stanley-Wilf conjecture: |Av_n(sigma)| <= c(sigma)^n for some c(sigma) finite
trivial_case.result: the Stanley-Wilf limit L(sigma) = lim |Av_n(sigma)|^{1/n} exists and is finite

open_case.description: Exact value of L(sigma) for general sigma of length k >= 4
open_case.result: exact: L(132)=4, L(1342)=8, L(1234)=9 (Regev 1981), L(1324) only known to be roughly in (10.27, 13.5) — exact value unknown
open_case.status: OPEN — L(1324) is the most famous open Stanley-Wilf limit

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims L(1324) has been computed (it has not — only bounds), OR conflates the existence-of-limit (proven) with exact-value (open for most patterns)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; existence-proven vs exact-value-open

entries.conjecture_family: Lyapunov exponents in the quadratic family

trivial_case.description: Positive Lyapunov exponent for a positive-measure set of parameters: for the real quadratic family x -> x^2 + c, there is a positive-measure set of c in [-2, 1/4] for which the critical orbit has positive Lyapunov exponent (Collet-Eckmann maps)
trivial_case.result: Existence of a positive-measure set of stochastic parameters with positive Lyapunov exponent
trivial_case.venue: Jakobson, Comm. Math. Phys. 81 (1981), 39-88; Benedicks-Carleson, Annals of Math. 122 (1985), 1-25

open_case.description: Sharp Lyapunov-exponent regularity / monotonicity along the family: is the Lyapunov exponent function c -> chi(c) continuous (or monotone) on the set of stochastic parameters? Is it analytic on hyperbolic components and Holder elsewhere?
open_case.result: Continuity at Misiurewicz parameters and along certain families is known (Tsujii, Avila-Moreira); full regularity / monotonicity question and behavior at the boundary of the stochastic set is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Jakobson / Benedicks-Carleson as 'fully describing Lyapunov exponents in the quadratic family', missing the regularity question
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish existence of positive-measure stochastic set (proven) from regularity of the Lyapunov-exponent function (open)

trivial_case.description: Lebesgue differentiation theorem: for f in L^1_loc(R^n), the average over balls B_r(x) tends to f(x) as r -> 0 for a.e. x
trivial_case.result: a.e. differentiation

open_case.description: Differentiation along arbitrary bases of convex sets in R^n: sharp class for differentiation along homothetically-shrinking convex bases (Falconer / Stein-style differentiation problems)
open_case.result: various restricted bases settled (rectangles in product form via Jessen-Marcinkiewicz-Zygmund 1935); fully arbitrary convex bases differentiation is partially open at the sharp regularity exponent
open_case.status: OPEN in some specific basis classes

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims differentiation results extend trivially to arbitrary bases (Saks's example shows differentiation can fail for L^1 along rectangles with arbitrary orientation)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; classical theorem doesn't extend to arbitrary geometric bases

trivial_case.description: Hadamard matrices exist for all orders 2^k (Sylvester 1867)
trivial_case.result: explicit construction H_{2^k} = block recursion of H_1 = (1)

open_case.description: Hadamard conjecture: Hadamard matrix exists for every order divisible by 4
open_case.result: verified for all orders <= 664 and many others; smallest open frontier shifted with Kharaghani-Tayfeh-Rezaie 2005 resolving 668

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Hadamard conjecture is 'almost surely true' as if proven, OR attributes Hadamard to Sylvester (Sylvester gave the 2^k construction; the general conjecture is Hadamard 1893)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; partial-construction vs general-existence-open

trivial_case.description: Lusin's separation theorem: any two disjoint analytic sets in a Polish space can be separated by a Borel set
trivial_case.result: Classical descriptive-set-theoretic theorem, proved by Souslin's operation analysis
trivial_case.venue: Lusin, 'Leçons sur les ensembles analytiques' (1930); separation theorem in 1927 papers

open_case.description: Whether the projective separation property at level Pi^1_n for n >= 3 holds in ZFC alone (without determinacy/large cardinal hypotheses); equivalently, whether ZFC proves the Pi^1_3 separation property
open_case.result: Under PD, projective separation holds with periodicity (Moschovakis); in ZFC alone, Pi^1_2 separation holds (Kondo's uniformization theorem 1939) but Pi^1_n for n >= 3 requires hypotheses beyond ZFC
open_case.status: OPEN: precise level at which separation requires large cardinals/determinacy is studied but not fully classified in ZFC alone

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims projective separation at Pi^1_3 follows from ZFC; conflates settled analytic separation (Lusin 1927) with open higher-projective levels
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish ZFC-provable analytic separation from open higher-projective levels

trivial_case.description: Falconer distance set conjecture in R^2: if E in R^2 has dim_H E > 1, then the distance set Delta(E) := {|x-y| : x,y in E} has positive Lebesgue measure
trivial_case.result: Conjecturally yes if dim_H > 1; dim_H > 1 is necessary (counterexample at exactly dim 1)
trivial_case.proven_by: various: Wolff 1999 dim_H > 4/3; Guth-Iosevich-Ou-Wang 2020 (paraphrase) dim_H > 5/4 in 2D; sharp threshold 1 still open
trivial_case.status: OPEN at sharp threshold 1 in 2D; partial bounds proven

open_case.description: Falconer conjecture in R^n, n >= 2 at sharp threshold dim_H E > n/2
open_case.result: Erdogan 2005 dim_H > n/2 + 1/3 (or similar partial bound); sharp threshold n/2 OPEN in all n >= 2

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Falconer distance set is 'proven by Wolff' or attributes the latest 5/4 bound to Falconer himself
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; conjecture name, partial bounds, sharp threshold all distinct

trivial_case.description: Yau conjecture upper bound: for the n-dim Laplacian eigenfunction with eigenvalue lambda, H^{n-1}(nodal set) <= C sqrt{lambda}
trivial_case.result: upper bound proven for analytic metrics by Donnelly-Fefferman 1988

open_case.description: Yau conjecture upper bound for C^infinity (smooth, non-analytic) metrics
open_case.result: Logunov-Malinnikova 2018-2019: H^{n-1}(nodal set) <= C lambda^{alpha} for some alpha < 1 (better than the trivial sqrt-lambda but weaker than Yau); sharp bound OPEN
open_case.status: OPEN in smooth (non-analytic) case at sharp Yau-conjectured rate

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Yau conjecture is 'proven by Donnelly-Fefferman' without analyticity restriction
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; regularity of metric flips status

entries.conjecture_family: Restriction conjecture (Stein, for the sphere S^{n-1})

trivial_case.description: L^2-extension endpoint: E: L^2(S^{n-1}) -> L^q(R^n) bounded for q >= 2(n+1)/(n-1) (Stein-Tomas range)
trivial_case.result: boundedness on the L^2-orthogonality range
trivial_case.venue: Bulletin of the American Mathematical Society (Tomas)

open_case.description: Full Stein restriction conjecture: E: L^q(S^{n-1}) -> L^q(R^n) bounded for all q > 2n/(n-1) in n >= 3 (q in (2n/(n-1), infinity])
open_case.result: n=2 settled by Carleson-Sjolin / Fefferman 1970; n >= 3 partial via bilinear (Tao-Vargas-Vega 1998), polynomial method (Guth 2016 Annals), decoupling (Hickman-Rogers 2019 paraphrase, Wang 2022 paraphrase); full conjecture OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates the proven Stein-Tomas endpoint with the full Stein conjecture, claiming restriction is 'proven by Tomas' without specifying the L^2 vs full range distinction.
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; Stein-Tomas is a proven endpoint within an open conjecture, name-collision risk high

trivial_case.description: Every Borel game on omega^omega with Borel payoff set is determined (one of the players has a winning strategy)
trivial_case.result: Borel determinacy in ZFC; the proof uses iterated power set up through the Borel hierarchy and is provably non-eliminable from ZFC (Friedman 1971 lower bound)

open_case.description: The exact consistency strength of long projective determinacy at length omega^2 (precise Woodin-cardinal placement) remains open; equivalence of PD with all its analytical consequences for projective sets (Woodin's last Delfino problem) is open
open_case.result: Pi^1_{n+1}-determinacy at length omega^2 implies model with omega+n Woodin cardinals (Aguilera-Mueller 2020 partial); upper-equivalence direction conjectural
open_case.status: OPEN; partial progress on long-game consistency strength; analytical-equivalence Delfino problem unresolved

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims 'projective determinacy is a ZFC theorem' (false — needs Woodin cardinals), or claims length-omega^2 consistency strength is exactly characterized (only one direction known)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish ZFC-provable Borel determinacy (Martin 1975) from large-cardinal-requiring PD and its open precise calibration

trivial_case.description: NLTS (No Low-energy Trivial States): there exist k-local Hamiltonian families with constant gap such that any state with energy within a constant of the ground state has Omega(log n) circuit depth
trivial_case.result: Proven via good quantum LDPC codes

open_case.description: Quantum PCP conjecture: constant-gap k-local Hamiltonian estimation is QMA-complete
open_case.result: Open; NLTS is necessary but not sufficient

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: conflating NLTS with full qPCP, claiming qPCP is settled because NLTS is
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5 architectural pattern: necessary-not-sufficient distinction is exactly what substrate must preserve

entries.conjecture_family: Propagation of chaos for interacting particle systems

trivial_case.description: McKean-Vlasov / smooth-kernel propagation of chaos: for N interacting diffusions with bounded Lipschitz interaction kernel, as N -> infinity the empirical measure converges to the McKean-Vlasov nonlinear PDE
trivial_case.result: Quantitative propagation of chaos with Lipschitz drift
trivial_case.venue: Sznitman, 'Topics in propagation of chaos', Ecole d'Ete de Probabilites de Saint-Flour XIX-1989, Springer LNM 1464

open_case.description: Propagation of chaos for 2D point-vortex / singular Biot-Savart kernel: derive 2D Euler / Navier-Stokes from N interacting point vortices in the singular regime
open_case.result: Partial results: Fournier-Hauray-Mischler 2014 for 2D Navier-Stokes with viscosity; Goodman-Hou-Lowengrub for vanishing viscosity; full singular case for 2D Euler from deterministic vortices is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Sznitman as 'establishing propagation of chaos' without distinguishing Lipschitz kernels (proven) from singular kernels like Biot-Savart (partial)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish smoothness regime of interaction kernel within the propagation-of-chaos program

entries.conjecture_family: Persistence of invariant tori for nearly integrable Hamiltonians

trivial_case.description: Diophantine tori in real-analytic, nondegenerate, nearly integrable Hamiltonians: Diophantine invariant tori of the integrable system persist under small real-analytic perturbations satisfying Kolmogorov nondegeneracy
trivial_case.result: Classical KAM theorem: positive-measure set of Diophantine tori survives small perturbations
trivial_case.venue: Kolmogorov, Dokl. Akad. Nauk SSSR 98 (1954); Arnold, Russian Math. Surveys 18 (1963); Moser, Nachr. Akad. Wiss. Gottingen (1962)

open_case.description: Arnold diffusion conjecture: for generic real-analytic perturbations of a nondegenerate integrable Hamiltonian with n >= 3 degrees of freedom, there exist orbits whose action variables undergo O(1) drift along resonant channels in the gaps between KAM tori
open_case.result: Examples constructed (Mather, Cheng-Yan, Kaloshin-Zhang and others) for specific systems; full genericity for n >= 3 with explicit time bounds and topological generality is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model presents KAM as 'solving stability of nearly integrable systems', missing that KAM gives stability ON tori but Arnold diffusion in the gaps is open
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish persistence of Diophantine tori (proven) from generic instability in the gaps (Arnold diffusion, open)

trivial_case.description: Monotone CLIQUE has super-polynomial monotone circuit complexity
trivial_case.result: Proven via approximation method; later strengthened to exponential

open_case.description: Super-polynomial non-monotone (P/poly) lower bounds for CLIQUE or any explicit NP function
open_case.result: Open; Tardos 1988 showed monotone-vs-non-monotone gap is exponential — monotone bounds do not lift

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming monotone lower bounds resolve P vs NP
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: monotone-only result does not transfer (the canonical case)

trivial_case.description: The Whitehead problem 'every Whitehead group is free' is independent of ZFC: Shelah showed it follows from V=L and is refuted by MA + ¬CH
trivial_case.result: Independence of Whitehead's conjecture from ZFC
trivial_case.venue: Israel J. Math. 18 (1974), 'Infinite abelian groups, Whitehead problem and some constructions'

open_case.description: Whether the Whitehead problem for groups of cardinality aleph_omega+1 (or other specific singular-successor cardinalities) has the same independence pattern as at aleph_1, or whether ZFC settles it at higher cardinalities under additional cardinal-arithmetic hypotheses
open_case.result: Eklof-Shelah and Magidor-Shelah extended independence to many cardinalities; the precise behavior at successors of singulars under SCH-failure scenarios is partially open
open_case.status: OPEN at specific singular-successor cardinalities under cardinal-arithmetic constraints

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Whitehead at aleph_1 is unsolved (it is solved — independent), or treats higher-cardinality cases as automatic from aleph_1 result
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled independence at aleph_1 (Shelah 1974) from open higher-cardinality refinements within the same family

trivial_case.description: EFL for small n
trivial_case.result: verified by Hindman 1981 and successors for n <= 10 and beyond computationally

open_case.description: EFL conjecture in full: union of n complete graphs each on n vertices, pairwise sharing at most one vertex, is n-colorable
open_case.result: Kang-Kelly-Kühn-Methuku-Osthus 2021 (arxiv 2101.04698) proved EFL for sufficiently large n
open_case.status: PROVEN for large n; small-to-large transition has unspecified threshold N_0

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims KKKMO 'proved EFL' without the 'sufficiently large n' caveat OR claims it remains entirely open
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; small-n-verified plus large-n-proved vs threshold-N_0-implicit

trivial_case.description: Birkhoff pointwise ergodic theorem: for a measure-preserving transformation T on (X, mu) and f in L^1, the time averages (1/N) sum_{n=0}^{N-1} f(T^n x) converge mu-a.e.
trivial_case.result: Birkhoff's pointwise ergodic theorem
trivial_case.venue: Birkhoff, 'Proof of the ergodic theorem', Proc. Nat. Acad. Sci. USA 17 (1931), 656-660

open_case.description: Pointwise convergence of multiple ergodic averages along polynomial iterates: for an ergodic m.p.s. and f, g in L^infty, does (1/N) sum_{n=1}^{N} f(T^{n} x) g(T^{n^2} x) converge mu-a.e.?
open_case.result: L^2 norm convergence proven (Furstenberg 1977 for n,2n; Host-Kra 2005, Ziegler 2007 for general linear; Bergelson-Leibman for polynomials in norm); pointwise a.e. convergence of f(T^n x) g(T^{n^2} x) is OPEN (recent partial results by Krause-Mirek-Tao 2022 for one polynomial in L^p, p > 1)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Birkhoff or Host-Kra L^2 convergence as resolving pointwise convergence of multiple polynomial averages
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish pointwise convergence of single Birkhoff average from pointwise convergence of multiple polynomial averages

trivial_case.description: MIP = NEXP (classical multi-prover interactive proofs equal NEXP)
trivial_case.result: Proven via two-prover one-round protocols + arithmetization

open_case.description: Quantitative qPCP-style results: constant-gap multi-prover quantum protocols at specific gap thresholds (the relationship between MIP* hierarchy levels at finite parameter regimes)
open_case.result: Largely open; MIP* = RE settled the unbounded-resource case 2020 but finer structure is open

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming MIP*=RE settles all multi-prover quantum questions
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: limit-result vs finite-parameter distinction

entries.conjecture_family: Harmonic measure dimension (Makarov problem and analogues)

trivial_case.description: Plane: harmonic measure on a simply connected domain in C has Hausdorff dimension 1 (Makarov 1985)
trivial_case.result: dim_H(omega) = 1

open_case.description: R^n, n >= 3: dimension of harmonic measure on a domain with arbitrary boundary
open_case.result: Bourgain 1987 proved dim_H(omega) <= n - delta_n for some delta_n > 0; sharp threshold open; Wolff 1995 / 2000s sharper bounds in specific cases
open_case.status: OPEN in n >= 3 (sharp value of n - delta_n)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model attributes the n>=3 results to Makarov, or claims Makarov's theorem applies to all dimensions
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; planar settled, higher-dim open

trivial_case.description: Sarnak's conjecture for zero-entropy systems with specific structure: Mobius disjointness holds for nilsystems, horocycle flows, certain rank-one systems, interval exchange transformations of bounded type, and various automatic / substitution systems
trivial_case.result: Mobius disjointness in many specific zero-entropy classes
trivial_case.venue: Green-Tao, Annals of Math. 175 (2012), 541-566; Bourgain-Sarnak-Ziegler, in 'From Fourier Analysis and Number Theory to Radon Transforms and Geometry', Springer 2013; Ferenczi-Mauduit-Rivat, J. London Math. Soc. 2017

open_case.description: General Sarnak's conjecture: for every zero-entropy topological dynamical system (X, T) and every continuous f: X -> C and every x in X, (1/N) sum_{n=1}^{N} f(T^n x) mu(n) -> 0, where mu is the Mobius function
open_case.result: Logarithmic version proved equivalent to Chowla by Tao 2017; full averaged form OPEN; Chowla itself OPEN; specific systems still resist (e.g., some smooth flows on surfaces)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites the nilsequence / horocycle special cases as 'proving Sarnak's conjecture'
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish specific-class Mobius-disjointness theorems from the general Sarnak conjecture

trivial_case.description: Almost-everywhere normality: with respect to Lebesgue measure on [0,1], almost every real number is normal in every integer base b >= 2
trivial_case.result: Borel's normal number theorem
trivial_case.venue: Borel, 'Les probabilites denombrables et leurs applications arithmetiques', Rend. Circ. Mat. Palermo 27 (1909), 247-271

open_case.description: Normality of specific algebraic irrationals: is sqrt(2) normal in base 10? More generally, is every algebraic irrational number normal in every base?
open_case.result: No algebraic irrational is known to be normal in any base; Borel's conjecture that they all are is OPEN; even a single digit-frequency lower bound for sqrt(2) of strength c log N (for the count of any fixed digit in the first N base-10 digits) is open

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates 'almost every real is normal' (Borel 1909) with 'sqrt(2) is normal' (open) - presence of normality somewhere implies it for specific algebraics
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish measure-theoretic 'almost every' (proven) from individual algebraic instances (open)

entries.conjecture_family: Pointwise convergence of Schrodinger-evolution to initial data (Carleson Schrodinger problem)

trivial_case.description: Schrodinger pointwise convergence in 1D: u(x, t) -> f(x) pointwise a.e. as t -> 0+ for f in H^s(R), s >= 1/4
trivial_case.result: s = 1/4 sharp (Sjolin-Vega 1988; Carleson 1980 conjectured)
trivial_case.proven_by: Sjolin (separately Vega) for the upper threshold; Dahlberg-Kenig 1982 gave the optimal s = 1/4

open_case.description: Schrodinger pointwise convergence in n >= 2: optimal s such that u(x, t) -> f(x) a.e. as t -> 0+ for f in H^s(R^n)
open_case.result: Bourgain 2016 disproved s = 1/4 sufficiency for n >= 2; Du-Guth-Li 2017 proved s = 1/3 in 2D using polynomial method; n >= 3 has weaker bounds; sharp threshold OPEN in n >= 3
open_case.status: OPEN; 2D settled by Du-Guth-Li 2017 at s = 1/3

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims s = 1/4 is sufficient in all dimensions (1D-only result extrapolated)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; dimension-dependent threshold; 1D solved, higher-dim partially solved (2D done 2017), 3D+ open

entries.conjecture_family: Furstenberg's xp-invariant measure conjectures and times-p-times-q

trivial_case.description: Single-map xp invariance: for p >= 2 and the map T_p: x -> p x mod 1 on R/Z, classification of T_p-invariant ergodic Borel probability measures is wide open; but for the specific subclass of T_p-invariant Bernoulli measures the classification is trivial (they are determined by a finite probability vector, with maximal entropy giving Lebesgue)
trivial_case.result: Bernoulli T_p-invariant measures are classified explicitly via their digit-distribution vector
trivial_case.proven_by: Folklore from Bernoulli theory; explicit in Furstenberg's writing

open_case.description: Classification of all T_p-invariant ergodic measures (single-map version): without joint invariance under T_q for q multiplicatively independent of p, classify all T_p-invariant ergodic Borel probability measures on R/Z
open_case.result: Hopelessly wild without a second invariance: there are uncountably many T_p-invariant ergodic measures (e.g., supported on T_p-invariant Cantor sets); no rigidity holds for the single-map case; the classification problem in any meaningful sense is OPEN / known to be intractable

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates Furstenberg ×p ×q rigidity (joint, partially proven) with single-map ×p classification (intractably wild). The trick is that the joint case is the rigid one; reducing to single-map breaks rigidity entirely
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish joint invariance under multiplicatively-independent maps (rigid, partially proven) from single-map invariance (wild, open / intractable). FM-08 risk: this looks like a 'simpler' version but is actually harder to classify

trivial_case.description: Robertson-Seymour Graph Minor Theorem: graphs are well-quasi-ordered under minor relation
trivial_case.result: every minor-closed family has a finite list of forbidden minors
trivial_case.venue: J. Combin. Theory Ser. B (multiple papers)

open_case.description: Polynomial-time recognition algorithms for specific minor-closed families with concrete constants
open_case.result: RS gave existence of O(n^3) recognition algorithms but with astronomical constants; Erdős-Pósa-style constants for specific families remain ongoing research
open_case.status: OPEN for many specific concrete-constant questions

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model presents RS GMT as 'gives polynomial-time algorithms for everything' without noting the constant explosion
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; existence-of-algorithm vs feasible-constants

trivial_case.description: Closed-set version: the only closed subsets of R/Z invariant under both x -> 2x mod 1 and x -> 3x mod 1 are finite sets of rationals and the entire circle
trivial_case.result: Closed-set (topological) rigidity for the joint x2, x3 action
trivial_case.venue: Math. Systems Theory 1, 1-49 ('Disjointness in ergodic theory, minimal sets, and a problem in Diophantine approximation')

open_case.description: Measure version assuming zero entropy: classify ergodic non-atomic Borel probability measures on R/Z jointly invariant under x2 and x3 with zero entropy under x2
open_case.result: Rudolph (1990) proved Lebesgue is the only such measure under the positive-entropy hypothesis; the zero-entropy case has no known nontrivial bound

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates closed-set rigidity (Furstenberg 1967) with measure rigidity (Rudolph 1990 + still-open zero-entropy case)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish topological from measure rigidity, and within measure rigidity distinguish positive-entropy (proven) from zero-entropy (open)

trivial_case.description: PFA implies 2^aleph_0 = aleph_2
trivial_case.result: Veličković and (independently) Todorčević used PFA-consequences (e.g., the P-ideal dichotomy / mapping reflection principle) to pin down c at aleph_2; classical Todorčević-Bekkali argument using OCA
