trivial_case.venue: Veličković, J. Symb. Logic 57 (1992); Todorčević, 'Partition Problems in Topology' AMS (1989)

open_case.description: Whether MM (Martin's Maximum) at omega_2 — i.e., a natural higher-cardinal analogue MM(aleph_2) preserving aleph_2-stationarity — is consistent and what continuum-function values it forces
open_case.result: MM++ implies (*) (Aspero-Schindler, Annals 2021), pinning c=aleph_2 from MM++. The MM(aleph_2) higher-cardinal analogue and its consistency strength remain under active investigation
open_case.status: OPEN; MM(aleph_2)-style axioms not yet shown consistent at full strength

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims MM(aleph_2) is known consistent or settled, or conflates PFA-implies-c=aleph_2 (settled) with higher-cardinal MM analogue (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish PFA pinning c (Veličković-Todorčević 1992) from open higher-cardinal forcing-axiom analogues

entries.conjecture_family: Stable solutions of semilinear elliptic equations (De Giorgi conjecture)

trivial_case.description: De Giorgi conjecture for stable monotone solutions of -Delta u = u - u^3 (Allen-Cahn) on R^n: in n <= 3, level sets are hyperplanes
trivial_case.result: n = 2 (Ghoussoub-Gui 1998); n = 3 (Ambrosio-Cabre 2000)
trivial_case.venue: Math. Ann. (Ghoussoub-Gui); J. Amer. Math. Soc. (Ambrosio-Cabre)

open_case.description: De Giorgi conjecture in n >= 4 (stable monotone solutions are 1D)
open_case.result: Savin 2009: n=4..8 proven under additional hypothesis; del Pino-Kowalczyk-Wei 2011 counterexample in n=9; full conjecture FALSE in n >= 9; SHARP version (n <= 8) holds under additional hypothesis
open_case.status: RESOLVED (false in n>=9; n=4..8 proven under extra hypothesis); sharper structural questions remain

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims De Giorgi conjecture is 'open in all dimensions n >= 4' or 'true in all dimensions' without distinguishing the resolution stage by stage
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; resolved in pieces with dimensional pieces having different status

entries.conjecture_family: Square principles and approachability at successors of singulars

trivial_case.description: If V=L, then square_kappa holds for every uncountable cardinal kappa (Jensen's square principle); hence square_aleph_omega holds in L, and similarly approachability AP(aleph_{omega+1}) holds
trivial_case.result: Jensen's fine-structural analysis of L; square_kappa is a global combinatorial principle in L
trivial_case.venue: Jensen, 'The fine structure of the constructible hierarchy', Annals of Math. Logic 4 (1972)

open_case.description: Whether the failure of square_aleph_omega together with SCH-failure at aleph_omega is consistent at strictly weaker large-cardinal strength than currently known (current: subcompact / strong compactness); precise consistency strength still being refined
open_case.result: Magidor-Shelah, Cummings-Foreman-Magidor, Sinapova-Unger (2018): consistency from supercompact; precise lower bound below subcompact open
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; precise consistency-strength calibration of square-failure + SCH-failure at aleph_omega is active

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims precise consistency strength of square-failure + SCH-failure at aleph_omega is known (only upper-bounded); conflates square in L (settled) with open consistency-calibration
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled square-in-L from open precise-strength of combined square+SCH failures

trivial_case.description: The constructible universe L exists in ZFC and is an inner model (transitive proper class containing all ordinals, satisfying ZFC). L satisfies V=L, GCH, and the diamond principle
trivial_case.result: L is defined by transfinite recursion using definable power set; ZFC proves L is a model of ZFC + GCH + diamond
trivial_case.venue: PNAS 24 (1938), 'The consistency of the axiom of choice and of the generalized continuum-hypothesis'

open_case.description: Construction of a fine-structural canonical inner model for one supercompact cardinal (the 'supercompact L'); the Sargsyan-Steel program and Woodin's Ultimate-L are active
open_case.result: Substantial partial progress: Sargsyan's hod mice through Lambda-mice levels (2025), Steel's K-construction at smaller large cardinals; supercompact level not yet achieved
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; recent Sargsyan-Steel 'Nairian models' (arXiv 2501.18958) is partial progress

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims canonical inner model for supercompact is constructed (false — only partial); conflates L (settled) with inner-model program (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled L (Goedel 1938) from open supercompact inner-model existence (Sargsyan-Steel program)

entries.conjecture_family: Permanent vs determinant in algebraic complexity (Valiant)

trivial_case.description: permanent is #P-complete; permanent is VNP-complete
trivial_case.result: Proven

open_case.description: VP != VNP (algebraic analog of P != NP)
open_case.result: Open

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming completeness implies separation
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: completeness-vs-separation distinction in algebraic complexity

trivial_case.description: Sobolev inequality W^{1,2}(R^n) -> L^{2n/(n-2)} sharp constant
trivial_case.result: explicit best constant via radial decreasing rearrangement
trivial_case.venue: J. Differential Geometry (Aubin); Annali di Mat. Pura ed Appl. (Talenti)

open_case.description: Sharp constants for higher-order Sobolev W^{k,p} -> L^q at general (k, p, q)
open_case.result: many specific cases settled (Beckner sharp Hausdorff-Young 1975; sharp Aubin-Talenti for W^{1,2}); general higher-order open in many regimes
open_case.status: OPEN in many specific (k, p, q) cases

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims 'sharp Sobolev constants are known' generically, conflating Aubin-Talenti's specific case with the general open question
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; specific case proven, generic case open

trivial_case.description: Specific small Ramsey numbers determined exactly
trivial_case.proven_by: various (Greenwood-Gleason 1955 for R(4,4); McKay-Radziszowski 1995 for R(4,5))

open_case.description: R(5,5) and beyond; asymptotic R(k,k)
open_case.result: R(5,5) bounded between 43 and 46 (Angeltveit-McKay 2017 upper); R(k,k) <= (4-2^{-7})^k (Campos et al. 2023 first exponential improvement on Erdős-Szekeres 4^k)
open_case.status: OPEN — R(5,5) exact value unknown for >70 years

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model fabricates specific R(5,5) value (42, 43, 45, 46 are all plausible-looking but R(5,5) is OPEN)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; small-cases-known vs medium-case-open

trivial_case.description: L^2 a.e. convergence: partial sums of Fourier series of f in L^2 converge a.e.
trivial_case.result: a.e. convergence

open_case.description: Sharp class for a.e. convergence at the L^1 endpoint: which class between L^1 and L log L log log L is sharp?
open_case.result: Antonov 1996: L log L log log L suffices; Konyagin 2000 (paraphrase): L log L counterexample possible; sharp class OPEN within this gap

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Carleson's theorem 'extends to all L^p, p >= 1' or attributes Antonov's refinement to Carleson
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; main theorem proven, sharp class refinement open

trivial_case.description: dc(perm_n) >= n^2 / 2 quadratic lower bound
trivial_case.result: Proven via Hessian-rank / second-derivative-dimension argument

open_case.description: Valiant's conjecture: dc(perm_n) is super-polynomial in n
open_case.result: Open; gap between Omega(n^2) lower bound and 2^n - 1 upper bound (Grenet)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the quadratic lower bound is super-polynomial
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: quantitative-but-not-asymptotic-strong distinction

trivial_case.description: The existence of an inaccessible cardinal is not provable in ZFC (assuming Con(ZFC)): if kappa is inaccessible then V_kappa models ZFC, so ZFC + 'exists inaccessible' proves Con(ZFC), violating Goedel's second incompleteness theorem
trivial_case.result: Standard application of Goedel-second-incompleteness via V_kappa modeling ZFC
trivial_case.proven_by: Folklore from Goedel/Shepherdson; rigorous treatment in Kanamori 'The Higher Infinite'
trivial_case.venue: Kanamori, 'The Higher Infinite' (Springer 2003); originally observed in the 1930s after Goedel

open_case.description: The HOD Conjecture (Woodin): assuming an extendible cardinal kappa, V is 'close to' HOD (specifically, every regular cardinal above kappa is omega-strongly measurable in HOD or HOD computes successors correctly). Recent work (exacting cardinals, Bagaria-Goldberg-Lucke 2024) suggests possible refutation
open_case.result: HOD Dichotomy theorem (Woodin 2017) establishes the dichotomy; HOD Conjecture (the 'close' alternative) remains open and recent work threatens it via exacting/ultraexacting cardinals
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; recent arXiv 2411.11568 (Bagaria-Goldberg-Lucke) argues exacting cardinals would refute HOD Conjecture

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims HOD Conjecture is settled (it is not) or claims inaccessibles are provable in ZFC (false)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled non-provability of inaccessibles from open HOD-Conjecture status

entries.conjecture_family: Reverse mathematics Big Five and Ramsey-type principles

trivial_case.description: Konig's lemma for finitely-branching trees (KL) is equivalent to ACA_0 over RCA_0; weak Konig's lemma WKL_0 is strictly weaker than ACA_0 over RCA_0
trivial_case.result: Big-Five hierarchy RCA_0 < WKL_0 < ACA_0 strictly increases; established via standard reverse-math techniques
trivial_case.venue: Simpson, 'Subsystems of Second-Order Arithmetic', Springer (1999); core results from 1970s-80s reverse-math program

open_case.description: The exact strength of Ramsey's theorem for pairs RT^2_2 over RCA_0: it is known to lie between RCA_0 and ACA_0, but its precise placement and whether it implies WKL_0 remain open
open_case.result: RT^2_2 does not imply ACA_0 (Seetapun-Slaman 1995); does not imply WKL_0 (Liu 2012); no natural principle is known strictly between ACA_0 and RT^2_2
open_case.status: OPEN: precise reverse-math placement of RT^2_2 still investigated; many sub-questions open

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims RT^2_2 is equivalent to ACA_0 (false — Seetapun-Slaman 1995), or places it at RCA_0/WKL_0 levels incorrectly
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled Big-Five strict hierarchy from open precise placement of RT^2_2

entries.conjecture_family: Hadwiger-Nelson chromatic number of the plane

trivial_case.description: Bounds 4 <= chi(R^2) <= 7 from the 1950s
trivial_case.result: lower bound 4 from explicit unit-distance graph; upper bound 7 from hexagonal tiling

open_case.description: Exact value of chi(R^2)
open_case.result: de Grey 2018 (arxiv 1804.02385) raised lower bound to 5; Polymath 16 refined to small explicit graphs; upper bound 7 unchanged

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims chi(R^2) is known to be 7 (the upper bound) OR claims de Grey proved it equals 5 (de Grey raised the lower bound to 5; the upper bound remains 7)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; bounds-known vs exact-unknown; de Grey's contribution is lower-bound only

trivial_case.description: Bochner-Riesz conjecture in dimension n=2
trivial_case.result: Proven for the full conjectured range of (delta, p)

open_case.description: Bochner-Riesz conjecture in n >= 3
open_case.result: Open; partial results via bilinear methods, decoupling

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the conjecture is proven (true only in n=2)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: dimensional threshold pattern

trivial_case.description: Latin square of order n exists for every n >= 1
trivial_case.result: trivial: cyclic shift Z_n gives one; many others

open_case.description: Existence of three Mutually Orthogonal Latin Squares (MOLS(n)) for all n != 6 and n != 2; the related combinatorial-design existence-of-resolvable Steiner triple systems
open_case.result: Chowla-Erdős-Straus 1960 / Bose-Shrikhande-Parker 1959 resolved Euler's '36 officers' OMOLS(6) impossibility and existence for n != 2,6; the 3-MOLS existence for various n is open or recently resolved (Keevash-Sah-Sawhney 2022 type results)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims '36 officers problem' is open (it is solved: no OMOLS(6) by Tarry 1900, Bose-Shrikhande-Parker 1959 for OMOLS existence n != 2, 6)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; classical-existence-resolved vs MOLS-extensions-some-open

trivial_case.description: ZFC proves aleph_1 <= b <= d <= 2^aleph_0 and aleph_1 <= cov(M) <= non(N) <= cof(N), giving the Cichon diagram inclusions
trivial_case.result: Cichon diagram of cardinal characteristics is fully established in ZFC; all 10 entries with their inequalities provably hold
trivial_case.venue: Bartoszyński-Judah, 'Set Theory: On the Structure of the Real Line' (1995)

open_case.description: Whether all 'reasonable' cardinal characteristics in Cichon's maximum (Goldstern-Kellner-Shelah 2019 showed all 10 distinct) extend to higher analogues at uncountable cardinals; whether a fully analogous diagram for kappa-Cichon at uncountable kappa is consistent
open_case.result: Goldstern-Kellner-Shelah (Annals 2022) established Cichon's maximum (10 distinct values); higher-cardinal generalizations are under active investigation, with several specific open consistencies
open_case.status: OPEN at uncountable cardinals; specific higher-Cichon configurations unresolved

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims higher-Cichon at uncountable cardinals is settled by analogy with omega; conflates Cichon's maximum (settled at omega) with open uncountable analogues
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled Cichon diagram and Cichon's maximum from open higher-cardinal generalizations

trivial_case.description: No wandering domains for rational maps of the Riemann sphere: every Fatou component of a rational map of degree >= 2 is eventually periodic (no wandering domains)
trivial_case.result: Sullivan's no-wandering-domains theorem for rational maps
trivial_case.venue: Sullivan, 'Quasiconformal homeomorphisms and dynamics I: Solution of the Fatou-Julia problem on wandering domains', Annals of Math. 122 (1985), 401-418

open_case.description: Wandering domains for transcendental entire functions: classify the dynamical / topological types of wandering domains, and determine whether wandering domains exist in all natural parameter families (e.g., Eremenko-Lyubich class B)
open_case.result: Existence of wandering domains for some transcendental entire functions known (Baker 1976); Bishop 2015 constructed wandering domains in class B; full classification of wandering-domain types and existence questions for several specific function families remain OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Sullivan as 'eliminating wandering domains in complex dynamics' without distinguishing rational (no wandering, proven) from transcendental (wandering exists; classification open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish rational maps (Sullivan, no wandering) from transcendental maps (Baker-Bishop, wandering exists; structure open)

trivial_case.description: Off-diagonal Ramsey R(3, k)
trivial_case.result: R(3,k) = Theta(k^2 / log k) (Kim 1995 lower bound matching Ajtai-Komlós-Szemerédi 1980 upper bound)

open_case.description: Off-diagonal R(4, k) tight asymptotics
open_case.result: best lower bound R(4,k) >= Omega(k^{5/2}/log^2 k) (Mattheus-Verstraete 2024); upper bound R(4,k) <= O(k^3 / log^2 k) (Ajtai-Komlós-Szemerédi); gap of k^{1/2}

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims R(4,k) is k^3/log^2 k tight (it is only the upper bound) OR conflates the recent Mattheus-Verstraete improvement with closing the gap
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; tight-bound vs gap-with-recent-progress

trivial_case.description: Under AD, every set of reals has the Baire property and is Lebesgue measurable; the Solovay model ZF+DC+'every set of reals is Lebesgue measurable' is consistent relative to an inaccessible cardinal
trivial_case.result: Solovay's construction via Levy collapse of an inaccessible; Mycielski-Steinhaus AD-implies-LM/BP
trivial_case.venue: Solovay, Annals of Math 92 (1970), 'A model in which every set of reals is Lebesgue measurable'

open_case.description: Whether AD_R (determinacy of all real-payoff games) + DC has the same consistency strength as 'AD_R + Theta is regular', and the precise calibration of long-game determinacy at length omega^2 with Woodin-cardinal hierarchies
open_case.result: Woodin established AD_R + Theta-regular has measurable-Woodin-cardinal-equivalence-strength; partial-progress on length-omega^2 (Aguilera, Mueller, Schlutzenberg, Trang 2020-2024); full classification open
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; arXiv 1906.11949 represents the 'long projective determinacy' partial progress

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims AD_R + Theta-regular has the same calibration as ordinary AD (it does not); conflates Solovay model (settled) with AD_R-strength refinements (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled Solovay model and AD-implications from open AD_R-Theta-regular calibration

trivial_case.description: Freiman's theorem: small-doubling A in Z embeds in a generalized arithmetic progression of bounded dimension and size
trivial_case.result: bounded-doubling implies bounded-dimension GAP containment
trivial_case.venue: monograph 'Foundations of a Structural Theory of Set Addition'

open_case.description: Polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa over Z: A with |A+A| <= K|A| is contained in a GAP of size <= poly(K) * |A| with dimension <= poly(log K)
open_case.result: Sanders 2012 quasi-polynomial; Gowers-Green-Manners-Tao 2023 (arxiv 2311.05762) proved PFR over F_2^n; PFR over Z is OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims PFR over Z is solved by GGMT 2023 (GGMT solved PFR over F_2^n; the integer version is still open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; F_2-version solved vs Z-version open

trivial_case.description: Upper bound r_3(F_3^n) <= O(2.756^n)
trivial_case.result: Proven via polynomial method / slice rank

open_case.description: Exact asymptotic constant for r_3(F_3^n) — closing the gap between Behrend-Edel-style lower bounds and the Ellenberg-Gijswijt 2.756 upper bound
open_case.result: Open; multiplicative gap of ~5x at n=7 between LB and UB

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming 2.756 is the tight asymptotic constant
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: bound-vs-tight-constant distinction

trivial_case.description: Reducibility of one-frequency Diophantine quasi-periodic cocycles in SL(2,R) close to constant: for alpha Diophantine and A in SL(2,R) with rotation number Diophantine w.r.t. alpha, the cocycle (alpha, A + small perturbation) is analytically reducible to a constant cocycle
trivial_case.result: Eliasson's reducibility theorem (one-frequency, near-constant, Diophantine)
trivial_case.venue: Eliasson, 'Floquet solutions for the 1-dimensional quasi-periodic Schrodinger equation', Comm. Math. Phys. 146 (1992), 447-482

open_case.description: Almost-reducibility / global theory for non-perturbative SL(2,R) cocycles with Liouville frequency: for Liouville alpha and arbitrary (not near-constant) analytic A, characterize the spectrum of reducible / almost-reducible / non-uniformly hyperbolic regimes
open_case.result: Avila's global theory and almost-reducibility theorem (Acta Math. 2015, Annals 2015) handle the one-frequency analytic case modulo the critical-set obstruction; the multi-frequency Liouville case and sharp boundary description for the critical set remain OPEN in many regimes

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Eliasson or Avila as resolving 'reducibility of quasi-periodic cocycles' without restricting to one-frequency / Diophantine / analytic
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish near-constant Diophantine reducibility (Eliasson) from global / multi-frequency / Liouville cases (open / partial)

trivial_case.description: n = 2: full Bochner-Riesz conjecture proven; T^delta bounded on L^p iff |1/p - 1/2| <= (delta + 1/2)/2
trivial_case.result: full conjectured boundedness region holds in n=2

open_case.description: n >= 3: Bochner-Riesz conjecture asks T^delta bounded on L^p iff |1/p - 1/2| <= (delta + 1/2)/n
open_case.result: partial: Tao 2003 bilinear improvements; Bourgain-Guth 2011; Lee-Vargas 2010; full conjecture remains open; tightest bounds via decoupling (Bourgain-Demeter 2015) are still loose at the conjectural endpoint

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model treats 2D Carleson-Sjolin as if it covers all n; or attributes the n>=3 partial bounds to 'Stein 1958' (Stein's thesis covered different ranges).
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; same conjecture name across dimensions, drastically different status

trivial_case.description: C^1 density of hyperbolicity for surface diffeomorphisms: in dimension 2, hyperbolic diffeomorphisms (Axiom A) are C^1 dense among diffeomorphisms with the property that there is no homoclinic tangency or heterodimensional cycle (in dim 2 the latter is vacuous)
trivial_case.result: Surface case: density of hyperbolicity in C^1 topology away from tangencies; specifically Pujals-Sambarino: every C^1-generic surface diffeomorphism is either hyperbolic or has a homoclinic tangency
trivial_case.venue: Pujals-Sambarino, 'Homoclinic tangencies and hyperbolicity for surface diffeomorphisms', Annals of Math. 151 (2000), 961-1023

open_case.description: Palis density conjecture in higher dimensions and stronger topology: in dim >= 3, every diffeomorphism is C^r-approximated (r >= 1) by either a hyperbolic system, one with a homoclinic tangency, or one with a heterodimensional cycle; and the C^r (r >= 2) version even on surfaces
open_case.result: Crovisier-Pujals proved the C^1 dichotomy in higher dim under partial hyperbolicity assumptions; full Palis conjecture for general C^1 diffeomorphisms in dim >= 3, and the C^r version for r >= 2 in any dimension, remain OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Pujals-Sambarino as resolving 'Palis' density conjecture' without restricting to surfaces and C^1
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must track dimension and regularity (C^1 vs C^r) within the Palis program

trivial_case.description: The halting problem H = {(M,x) : Turing machine M halts on input x} is undecidable; equivalently, 0' (the Turing jump of 0) is strictly above 0 in the Turing degrees
trivial_case.result: Diagonal argument: assuming a decider D for H, construct M_D that halts iff D says it does not, contradiction
trivial_case.venue: Proc. London Math Soc. (2) 42 (1936-37), 'On computable numbers, with an application to the Entscheidungsproblem'

open_case.description: Whether the Turing degrees of c.e. sets D_T have any non-trivial automorphism (beyond the identity); the bi-interpretability conjecture for the c.e. degrees remains open
open_case.result: Non-trivial automorphism existence for the c.e. degrees is open; partial results show definability of large classes (Nies-Shore-Slaman 1998, Slaman-Woodin program)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates settled undecidability of halting with the open structural question of automorphisms in the c.e. degree structure
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled undecidability (Turing 1936) from open automorphism/rigidity questions in the same degree-theoretic family

trivial_case.description: Worst-case to average-case reduction for SVP (Shortest Vector Problem) approximation
trivial_case.result: Ajtai 1996 / 2004 reduction: average-case SIS as hard as worst-case GapSVP

open_case.description: Quantum hardness of SVP / LWE under quantum reductions; quantum-secure cryptography from worst-case lattice problems
open_case.result: Partial — Regev 2009 LWE reduction; quantum-vs-classical gap details open

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming classical reductions transfer cleanly to quantum
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: classical-vs-quantum reduction distinction

trivial_case.description: MM++ implies Woodin's Pmax axiom (*); equivalently, MM++ decides H(omega_2)-theory in a Pmax-canonical way
trivial_case.result: Aspero-Schindler proof using a tower-forcing-+-iteration argument; settles a question open since the 1990s
trivial_case.venue: Annals of Mathematics 193 (2021), 'Martin's Maximum^++ implies Woodin's axiom (*)'

open_case.description: Whether PFA alone (without MM++) implies (*) — equivalently, whether PFA is sufficient to decide H(omega_2)-theory in the Pmax-canonical way; the question of whether PFA is consistent with ¬(*) is also open
open_case.result: Aspero-Schindler proof crucially uses MM++ stationary-set-preservation, which PFA lacks; whether the result extends to PFA is conjecturally negative but not proven

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims PFA implies (*) (false — only MM++ is known to); conflates settled MM++-implies-(*) with open PFA-vs-(*) question
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled MM++-implies-(*) (Aspero-Schindler 2021) from open PFA-vs-(*) question

trivial_case.description: The Suslin Hypothesis (no Suslin lines on R) is independent of ZFC: it follows from MA + ¬CH (Solovay-Tennenbaum 1971) and fails under V=L (diamond gives a Suslin tree, Jensen 1968)
trivial_case.result: Independence of SH from ZFC
trivial_case.proven_by: Jensen (failure under V=L, 1968) and Solovay-Tennenbaum (consistency of SH, 1971)
trivial_case.venue: Solovay-Tennenbaum, Annals of Math 94 (1971); Jensen, Trans AMS (1972)

open_case.description: Generalized Suslin Hypothesis at omega_2 (no aleph_2-Suslin trees) and its precise consistency strength under stronger forcing axioms; whether SH(aleph_2) is consistent assuming only weakly compact below remains open in some formulations
open_case.result: Laver-Shelah established SH(aleph_2) from a weakly compact; consistency-strength refinements and behavior under PFA/MM remain open at higher cardinals
open_case.status: OPEN at specific higher-cardinal generalizations; active research (Hayut, Magidor, Veličković)

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims SH(aleph_2) follows automatically from SH(aleph_1) settled-as-independent; conflates omega_1 (settled) with omega_2 (open in ZFC + intermediate large cardinals)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled SH-independence at omega_1 from open higher-cardinal generalizations

trivial_case.description: Vizing's theorem on chromatic index
trivial_case.result: chi'(G) is in {Delta(G), Delta(G)+1}

open_case.description: List-Coloring Conjecture (LCC): chi'_L(G) = chi'(G); equivalently, edge-list-chromatic number equals chromatic index
open_case.result: Galvin 1995 proved it for bipartite multigraphs; Kahn 2000 proved an asymptotic version with epsilon error; full LCC OPEN in general

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Galvin proved LCC in general (Galvin proved it for bipartite multigraphs)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; bipartite-case-by-Galvin vs general-LCC-open

trivial_case.description: Ramsey-Turán bound for triangle-free graphs of large independence number
trivial_case.result: for triangle-free graphs G on n vertices with alpha(G) = o(n), max edges is at most n^2 / 8 + o(n^2)

open_case.description: Ramsey-Turán density for K_4: max edges in K_4-free graph with alpha = o(n)
open_case.result: Erdős-Sós conjectured 1/8 * n^2; Sudakov 2003 confirmed upper bound matches lower bound for K_4 and certain other cases; full picture for K_t with t >= 5 remains open
open_case.status: OPEN for K_t with t >= 5 in some regimes

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model treats Ramsey-Turán as 'all done by Erdős-Sós', missing the K_t generalization gap
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; specific-result vs general-extension

entries.conjecture_family: Singular Cardinals Hypothesis variants and PCF bounds

trivial_case.description: If kappa is singular of uncountable cofinality and 2^lambda = lambda^+ for all sufficiently large lambda < kappa, then 2^kappa = kappa^+ (GCH cannot first fail at a singular of uncountable cofinality)
trivial_case.result: Stationary-set reflection forces GCH to lift through uncountable-cofinality singulars; Silver's theorem
trivial_case.venue: Proc. ICM Vancouver 1974, 'On the singular cardinals problem'

open_case.description: Whether 2^aleph_omega can be precisely characterized in ZFC under the strong-limit hypothesis (Shelah's PCF gives 2^aleph_omega < aleph_{omega_4}; Shelah Strong Hypothesis pp(aleph_omega) < aleph_{omega_1} remains open)
open_case.result: Shelah Strong Hypothesis conjectures pp(aleph_omega) < aleph_{omega_1}; current ZFC bound 2^aleph_omega < aleph_{omega_4}
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; no improvement to Shelah's PCF bound has been confirmed

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model conflates Silver's uncountable-cofinality theorem with the (open) countable-cofinality case at aleph_omega; or claims SH/SCH is a theorem rather than a conjecture
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish proven uncountable-cofinality case (Silver 1974, technique=Fodor/regression) from open countable-cofinality case (PCF, technique=Shelah)

trivial_case.description: Friedberg-Muchnik theorem: there exist incomparable c.e. (recursively enumerable) Turing degrees strictly between 0 and 0', solving Post's problem
trivial_case.result: Priority method: simultaneous finite-injury construction of A and B with A not <=_T B and B not <=_T A

open_case.description: Whether the c.e. Turing degrees R have any non-trivial automorphism (the c.e. degrees automorphism problem); equivalently, the bi-interpretability conjecture for R
open_case.result: Slaman-Woodin program established many definability results; Nies-Shore-Slaman (1998) showed every relation invariant under automorphisms is definable; existence of a single non-trivial automorphism remains open

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims c.e. degrees are rigid (proved-no-automorphisms) when only partial automorphism-invariance results are known; conflates Post's problem (settled) with automorphism question (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled Post's-problem solution from open c.e.-degree-automorphism question

trivial_case.description: Four Color Theorem: every planar graph is 4-colorable
trivial_case.result: chi(G) <= 4 for planar G
trivial_case.proven_by: Appel, Haken (1976; computer-assisted); Robertson-Sanders-Seymour-Thomas (1997 simplified proof)

open_case.description: Hadwiger conjecture for k >= 7: chi(G) >= k implies K_k is a minor of G
open_case.result: open for k >= 7; reduced to 4CT for k = 6 by Robertson-Seymour-Thomas 1993

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model treats Hadwiger as 'proven up to k=6' as if k=7+ is incremental; the structural barrier at k=7 is qualitatively different (no 4CT-style reduction known)
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; planar-coloring vs general-Hadwiger

entries.conjecture_family: Vaught's conjecture and counting countable models

trivial_case.description: Vaught's conjecture for omega-stable theories: every countable complete omega-stable theory has either at most countably many or exactly 2^aleph_0 countable models
trivial_case.result: Shelah's stability-theoretic analysis using prime models over independent sets
trivial_case.venue: Shelah-Harrington-Makkai, Israel J. Math. 49 (1984), 'A proof of Vaught's conjecture for omega-stable theories'

open_case.description: Vaught's conjecture in full generality (for all countable first-order theories): the number of countable models is finite, aleph_0, or 2^aleph_0
open_case.result: Knight (2002, 2007) announced a counterexample, but it has not been verified by the community as of 2026; conjecture proven for many sub-classes (o-minimal, weakly minimal, superstable of finite U-rank), but unverified in general
open_case.status: OPEN as of 2026; status of Knight's claimed counterexample remains unresolved

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model claims Vaught's conjecture is settled in general (false — Knight's claim unverified, conjecture open); conflates omega-stable case (settled) with general case (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish settled omega-stable Vaught (Shelah 1984) from open general Vaught's conjecture

trivial_case.description: Sets of fractional dimension: rectifiability characterizations of curves of finite length
trivial_case.result: Riesz brothers theorem (1916): if Omega is a Jordan domain in C with rectifiable boundary, then harmonic measure is mutually absolutely continuous with arc-length measure

open_case.description: Higher-dim analog: characterization of rectifiable sets by harmonic measure absolute continuity in n >= 3
open_case.result: Azzam-Mourgoglou-Tolsa series 2015-2020: characterized in some cases (uniformly rectifiable Ahlfors regular boundaries); full case OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model attributes the higher-dim characterization to F. and M. Riesz
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; planar settled by classical names, higher-dim recent + open

trivial_case.description: Khintchine recurrence for a single nontrivial power: for an ergodic measure-preserving system (X, mu, T) and any A with mu(A) > 0, the set { n : mu(A intersect T^{-n} A) > mu(A)^2 - epsilon } has bounded gaps
trivial_case.result: Khintchine recurrence, single intersection
trivial_case.venue: Khintchine, 'Eine Verscharfung des Poincareschen "Wiederkehrsatzes"', Compositio Math. 1 (1934), 177-179

open_case.description: Khintchine-type lower bound for triple intersections with polynomial iterates: for all A, do we have { n : mu(A intersect T^{-n^2} A intersect T^{-2n^2} A) > mu(A)^c - epsilon } syndetic for some c?
open_case.result: Bergelson-Host-Kra 2005 proved Khintchine-type bounds for linear length-3 progressions; for length 4 it FAILS (Bergelson-Host-Kra counterexample); polynomial-iterate Khintchine-type lower bounds remain OPEN in general

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model assumes Khintchine-type bounds extend to all multiple recurrence, missing that they fail at length 4 and are open for polynomial iterates
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish single-return Khintchine (1934) from multiple-return Khintchine-type, and within multiple-return distinguish proven (length 3 linear), false (length 4 linear), and open (polynomial)

entries.conjecture_family: Helly-type theorems / convex geometry meets harmonic analysis

trivial_case.description: Mahler conjecture: lower bound on volume product Vol(K) Vol(K*) where K is a symmetric convex body in R^n
trivial_case.result: n=2 proven (Mahler himself 1939); n=3 proven (Iriyeh-Shibata 2020 paraphrase)
trivial_case.venue: Casopis (Mahler 1939); Duke Math. J. or paraphrase venue (Iriyeh-Shibata)

open_case.description: Mahler conjecture in n >= 4: lower bound on volume product
open_case.result: Bourgain-Milman 1987 gave lower bound c^n Vol(B^n)^2; Kuperberg 2008 improved constant; sharp Mahler bound OPEN for n >= 4

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model attributes general-n Mahler proof to Mahler himself, or claims Bourgain-Milman bound is the sharp Mahler bound
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; dimensional staging

trivial_case.description: Boson sampling and IQP sampling: classical hardness conditional on PH not collapsing
trivial_case.result: Proven (Aaronson-Arkhipov 2011 / Bremner-Jozsa-Shepherd 2010)

open_case.description: Verifiable quantum supremacy in the cryptographic-assumption-free setting
open_case.result: Open; Yamakawa-Zhandry 2024-class results give certified randomness under specific assumptions

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: conflating sampling-supremacy (proven conditional) with verifiable-supremacy (open)
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: sampling-vs-decision distinction

entries.conjecture_family: Conjugacy of circle diffeomorphisms to rotations

trivial_case.description: Denjoy theorem for C^2 circle diffeomorphisms with irrational rotation number: every C^2 orientation-preserving diffeomorphism of S^1 with irrational rotation number alpha is topologically conjugate to the rigid rotation R_alpha
trivial_case.result: Denjoy's theorem on topological conjugacy
trivial_case.venue: Denjoy, 'Sur les courbes definies par les equations differentielles a la surface du tore', J. Math. Pures Appl. 11 (1932), 333-375

open_case.description: Smooth conjugacy at the boundary of Diophantine: Herman-Yoccoz showed C^infty conjugacy holds when alpha is Diophantine; sharp regularity / loss-of-derivatives results across the full Diophantine-Liouville spectrum (e.g., precise Holder exponent of conjugacy as a function of arithmetic of alpha for finitely-differentiable diffeomorphisms) are OPEN in several regimes
open_case.result: Herman 1979, Yoccoz 1984 settled smooth conjugacy under Diophantine; Katznelson-Ornstein, Khanin-Sinai gave sharper conditions; precise regularity theory for finitely-differentiable diffeomorphisms with Liouville rotation numbers and the optimal arithmetic-vs-regularity Diophantine threshold are OPEN

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Denjoy / Herman-Yoccoz as fully resolving smooth conjugacy of circle diffeos, missing the finitely-differentiable / Liouville regime
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish topological conjugacy (Denjoy) from smooth conjugacy (Herman-Yoccoz) and from sharp finite-regularity questions (open)

trivial_case.description: Erdős-Rado sunflower lemma: family of k-sets with > (r-1)^k * k! sets contains a sunflower with r petals
trivial_case.result: (r-1)^k * k! upper bound

open_case.description: Erdős-Rado sunflower conjecture: c(r)^k bound (constant base instead of factorial)
open_case.result: Alweiss-Lovett-Wu-Zhang 2019 (arxiv 1908.08483) brought it to (C log k)^k for r=3; conjectured constant base

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model presents ALWZ bound as proving the conjecture, or attributes the original lemma to the modern improvers
entries.doctrine_relevance: FM-08; trivially-proven lemma vs open conjecture within sunflower theory

trivial_case.description: Real Fatou conjecture for the quadratic family: hyperbolic parameters are dense in the real interval of the Mandelbrot family x -> x^2 + c, c in R
trivial_case.result: Density of hyperbolicity holds in the real quadratic family; equivalently real polynomials in the quadratic family are densely hyperbolic
trivial_case.venue: Lyubich, Annals of Math. 149 (1999), 319-420; Graczyk-Swiatek, Annals of Math. 146 (1997), 1-52

open_case.description: Complex Fatou conjecture: hyperbolic components are dense in the parameter space of complex polynomials of degree d (already open for the complex quadratic family x -> x^2 + c, c in C, equivalent to MLC for that family)
open_case.result: MLC and density of hyperbolicity are open even in the complex quadratic family; MLC is known on a large set (Lyubich, Kahn-Lyubich) but not at all parameters

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: model cites Lyubich / Graczyk-Swiatek as resolving 'the Fatou conjecture' without flagging that the real-line case is what was proved, while the complex case (and MLC) remain open
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish density of hyperbolicity along the real slice from density in the full complex parameter space

trivial_case.description: k-Local Hamiltonian for k=2 is QMA-complete
trivial_case.result: Proven (with inverse-polynomial promise gap)

open_case.description: Constant-gap Local Hamiltonian is QMA-complete (qPCP)
open_case.result: Open; this is the quantum PCP conjecture

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming QMA-completeness implies qPCP
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: gap-strength is the load-bearing distinction (inverse-poly proven, constant open)

trivial_case.description: Yao's XOR lemma: if f is mildly hard (e.g. cannot be computed by size-s circuits with prob > 1/2 + delta), then f^{XOR k} is much harder
trivial_case.result: Proven (Yao 1982)

open_case.description: Optimal hardness amplification for NP (without circuit-LB hypothesis); equivalence between worst-case and average-case hardness for NP
open_case.result: Open; barriers in Bogdanov-Trevisan 2006

entries.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming XOR lemma implies arbitrary worst-to-average reductions for NP
entries.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: hardness-amplification scope
